They thrive in a creative and innovative environment like SXSW which affords countless opportunities to meet interesting people. In its third year at SXSW, the Superconnectors Meet Up is intentionally designed to give business development professionals a place to network with other business leaders.

events.name: The RAI of Every Day Things - from Principles to UX Practice
events.description: Responsible AI isn’t a buzzword—it’s a daily practice that drives the appropriate level of trust, mitigates risk, and powers better business outcomes. But what does it look like in product and design? It’s a practical, human-centered approach to designing AI systems that are safe, equitable, and effective. In this session, RAI leaders from Microsoft, SAP, Adobe, and JPMorgan Chase share real tools, UX strategies, and lessons from the trenches. Gain an understanding of RAI - and walk away with actionable best practices to apply now, whether you're leading solo or scaling across an enterprise.

events.name: AI: Friend or Foe of DEIB?
events.description:
Headlines warn that AI is hurting DEIB—propagating bias, reinforcing stereotypes, and risking the erasure of marginalized groups and their histories. Yet the same technology has the potential to uncover bias, scale representation, and raise the bar for inclusion at the brand, industry, academic, and societal levels.

With a consumer-focused lens, panelists from MAC Cosmetics, the UN, NYU, and Responsible AI company, SeeMe Index, will explore both sides of the equation: where AI’s blind spots have failed us, where it’s driving progress, and what it takes to ensure technology reflects the world we want to see.

events.name: Funding/Artist Support Mentor Session: Emily Best (Seed&Spark)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Producers/Production, Publicity & Marketing, "Sales, Acquisitions, & Distribution".

events.name: The Storytelling Paradox: Reflect, Refract, or Revolutionize
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description: Storytellers reveal truths that connect us—uncovering hidden patterns, holding up mirrors to society, exploring our shared humanity, and amplifying voices at the margins. Individual stories gain power when they accumulate, from the #MeToo movement to documentaries that change how we see the world. But decades of human choices—about technology, distribution, and who profits -—have left storytellers without basic infrastructure to tell stories that matter. How do storytellers preserve their power to reveal, connect, and transform?

events.name: See Where the Money Goes: Rebuild Trust in Humanitarian Aid
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description:
Trust is under pressure across industries, but in the humanitarian sector it has become existential. As public skepticism around waste, corruption, and opaque systems grows, support for aid can disappear fast—as seen in 2025 with sweeping cuts to U.S. foreign assistance driven by accountability concerns.

This session explores how blockchain is reshaping humanitarian aid by enabling real-time, verifiable tracking of stablecoin-based funds from donor to recipient. We’ll examine what trust-by-design systems mean not just for aid, but for any sector grappling with accountability at scale.

events.name: Mentor Session: Emily Gupton (SKG)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in "Diversity, Equity & Inclusion", Entrepreneurship, Future of Work.

events.name: The Consumer’s Guide to Beating the Bots
events.description: Corporations are weaponizing your personal data to inflate prices, suppress wages, and undermine transparency and basic fairness. This panel will expose how a convergence of corporate power and new technology has created a dizzying array of deceptive pricing practices. From surveillance pricing on airfares to algorithmic price fixing by landlords, companies are leveraging troves of consumer data to rip off customers. Our panelists will share how they have successfully defeated some of these nefarious practices and what policymakers, regulators, and consumers can do to fight back.

events.name: Building the World We Believe In
events.description: This panel brings together leaders from mission-driven organizations to discuss how they are using business as a force for good. Panelists will share real-world stories about creating impact, building values-driven cultures, and partnering across sectors to address complex challenges. The conversation will highlight innovative models and practical lessons for anyone seeking to create meaningful, lasting change.

events.name: It Writes Itself: A Live AI Comedy Variety Hour
events.description: A fast-moving, comedy experiment where performers, the audience, and Superhuman co-create live on stage. With sketches, music, storytelling, games, and surprises, the show writes itself in real time. Anything can happen. Chaos encouraged.Hosted by Mike Jacobson & Sami Kriegstein JacobsonGuest appearances by Nick Rutherford, Eric Andre & Maylee Todd

events.name: Zero-Gravity, Zero Barriers: Future of Inclusive Spaceflight
events.description: Discover what true Space accessibility means at this groundbreaking SXSW panel. Experts—including the first deaf Asian female pilot and aspiring astronaut, the founder of the Blind Institute of Technology, and a Space leader championing disability inclusion—reveal what it will take to make Space open to all. Explore innovations in pilot training, zero-gravity research, AI-assisted interfaces, and adaptive prosthetics. Learn how inclusive Space design inspires broader accessibility on Earth, reshaping our vision of who belongs in the final frontier.

name: Eric Krueger
title: Head of Platform and Developer Strategy

events.name: Mentor Session: Eric Krueger (Meta)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in XR, Immersive Technology, Content Strategy/Production.

events.name: The Future of Live Music: Immersive and Interactive
events.description:
The future of live music is interactive, immersive, and powered by real-time technology. This panel explores how concerts are evolving across physical and digital spaces through XR platforms, Unreal Engine, virtual performance, and production design.

Leaders from Meta, Magnopus, Epic Games, and Wave will share how they are transforming concerts into multi-sensory experiences, blending creativity and technology to shape the next generation of live performance.

events.name: The Next Evolution of XR & Shared Realities in Entertainment
events.description: Iconic worlds are being reimagined across XR and shared-reality formats, giving audiences new ways to step inside the stories they love. Bringing together leaders from Meta, Universal Pictures, Nexus Studios and Cosm, we’ll explore how spatial storytelling is evolving as major IP moves into these new environments – and what’s ahead as these mediums step from the bleeding-edge into the mainstream. The panel will unpack what audiences are responding to, how scalable business models are emerging across platforms, and the new creative opportunities this opens up for worldbuilders.

events.name: The Future of Development: Anthropic x Bolt.new A Case Study
events.description: Bolt.new went from $0 to $40M ARR in five months after launching on Claude 3.5 Sonnet — making it the fastest-growing customer in Anthropic's history. In this fireside chat, Bolt.new's CEO and CTO join Anthropic's Head of Applied AI to explore how AI is reshaping software development. They'll discuss the rise of "vibe coding," whether learning to code still matters, how the developer role is evolving from implementer to architect, and what's next as enterprise adoption accelerates. Expect candid takes on AI hype, scaling laws, technical debt, and what transformative AI actually looks like in production.

events.name: Resilience Through Innovation: AI at NPR and KQED
events.description: The loss of federal funding and AI's reshaping of the media landscape present risks and opportunities for public media and public service journalism. This session features a transparent conversation between a local and national leader who are experimenting with GAI in ways that maintain trust with the audience, center the work and judgment of journalists, and augment storytelling and engagement. Join us for a lively discussion focused on transparency, ethics and how organizations can advocate for the sustainability, impact, and continued production of high-quality, human-centered journalism.

events.name: Mentor Session: Esteban Contreras (Fleetio)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Product Management/Development, Marketing/Advertising/PR, Storytelling.

events.name: Where Should We Begin? Live with Esther Perel
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description: Join iconic psychotherapist Esther Perel for a special live-taping of her acclaimed podcast, Where Should We Begin?, where she speaks with real people in search of insight baring the raw, intimate, and profound details of their stories.

events.name: Resilience Through Innovation: AI at NPR and KQED
events.description: The loss of federal funding and AI's reshaping of the media landscape present risks and opportunities for public media and public service journalism. This session features a transparent conversation between a local and national leader who are experimenting with GAI in ways that maintain trust with the audience, center the work and judgment of journalists, and augment storytelling and engagement. Join us for a lively discussion focused on transparency, ethics and how organizations can advocate for the sustainability, impact, and continued production of high-quality, human-centered journalism.

events.name: Who Predicts Better and Why? Humans, AI, and the Anatomy of a Forecast
events.description: AI models process vast signals; humans bring judgment and context. But who calls real-world outcomes better, and when? This session compares human and model forecasts on headline events, drawing on newsroom practice, product experience, and reasoning-centric AI research. We’ll map where models’ pattern breadth wins, where human context does, and why hybrids often work best. Instead of scoreboards alone, we’ll discuss how journalists and analysts should use uncertainty ranges, disclose methods and sources, and avoid feedback loops that can steer the story itself.

events.name: Two Futurists and an AI: Seeing Trouble Ahead
events.description: Join celebrity futurist Faith Popcorn, techno-futurist Sarah DaVanzo, and Delph.Ai, an AI Synthetic Human trained on the minds of female futurists in a boundary-pushing, three-way debate on the future of leadership in the age of intelligent machines. With Faith’s dystopian view, Sarah’s utopian counterpoint, and Delph.Ai’s unexpected input, this session explores the trouble coming as AI challenges the very definition of human agency, influence, and power.

events.name: Mentor Session: Felipe Cornejo (Devsu)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Business Development, Entrepreneurship, Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning.

events.name: Agents at Work: Build AI That Ships with v0
events.description: Vibe coding isn't just for demos and landing pages anymore. In this hands-on workshop, the team behind v0 by Vercel will show you how to use natural language to build AI agents that actually transform how you work, inspired by agents already running in production at Vercel. You'll build your own and walk away with something real you can bring back to your team. No coding background needed. Just bring a problem worth solving, and we'll help you ship the solution.

events.name: Early Days Again: What AI Makes Possible for New Schools
events.description:
AI tools are improving quickly, and educators are already finding meaningful uses for them. What hasn’t been defined yet is the school model that makes AI work for students and teachers in a coherent, reliable way at scale.

This session examines what early adopters of AI in public education are learning in real classrooms and school systems, the concrete design choices and conditions behind early success, and the operational, instructional, and governance questions that must be resolved over the next five years for AI to strengthen public education rather than fragment it.

events.name: U.S. vs. The World: the Future We’re (Currently) Losing
events.description: Is the United States losing its edge in global innovation? In this high-energy continuation of last year’s SXSW session, Congressman Gabe Amo and VC Matthew Weinberg delve further into how Trump-era research cuts, clean energy rollbacks, and talent restrictions have created lasting consequences for U.S. competitiveness and opened the door for China to surge ahead. Blending policy insight with on-the-ground investor perspective, this advanced fireside chat asks: How do we revamp a system with no Plan B, and what bold, bipartisan moves can win our future back before it’s too late?

events.name: AI with Aloha: Stories from Hawai‘i’s Classrooms
events.venue: The Westin Austin Downtown - Paramount III
events.description: While most schools banned AI when it first emerged, one school in Hawai‘i saw the wave coming and started paddling early. As the emerging technology teacher at ‘Iolani School, my goal was to make every mistake as quickly as possible so that we can learn valuable lessons together. We knew from day one that AI was here to stay, so while others watched from shore, my students and I were wiping out, experimenting, and figuring it out together. This session shares the stories from those chaotic early days and how they led to the AI literacy programs we’ve built in Hawai‘i today.

events.name: Why SMB Cyber Attacks Could Cripple the U.S. Economy
events.description: Small and mid-sized businesses (SMBs) account for 99.9% of U.S. businesses and nearly half of GDP, yet their cybersecurity capabilities lag dangerously behind. Nation-state actors and cybercriminals now target SMBs and the managed service providers (MSPs) that support them because one breach can cascade across thousands of businesses, posing a serious risk to national security and economic stability. As AI supercharges social engineering and ransomware at scale, this overlooked attack surface has become one of the most urgent vulnerabilities in America.

events.name: Space Workforce Meet Up
events.description: Texas has always been the frontier of America’s biggest ideas - from cattle drives to microchips, music revolutions to moonshots. In 2026, the frontier is shifting upward, and the call for the New Space Cowboy is here.  In this era of commercial space, the next giant leap won’t be driven by astronauts, but welders, coders, machinists, technicians, scientists, mission operators, and dreamers. And no place on Earth is better positioned to cultivate this next gen of talent. Join the Space Workforce Incubator for Texas for a meetup that blends the spirit of the Wild West with the future's promise.

events.name: AI in Entertainment: Navigating IP, Ethics & Opportunity
events.description: Explore how generative and agentic AI are transforming entertainment and media, from content creation to distribution.  This session will unpack the legal, ethical, and business challenges—especially around IP, fair use, and talent rights—while highlighting new opportunities for innovation and collaboration.

events.name: The Cold Outreach Playbook for Tech Sales
events.description: Standing out in a crowded inbox or on a cold call is one of the toughest challenges in B2B sales. In this session, Glenn Kramon, Pulitzer Prize winning New York Times editor and Stanford Business School instructor, shares proven communication strategies that help sales professionals earn attention and credibility with decision-makers. You’ll learn how to open with impact, tailor messages to business priorities, remove unnecessary jargon, and strike the right balance of professionalism and authenticity to drive meaningful responses.

events.name: Lose Your Mind to AI? A Neuroscientist and an AI CEO Debate
events.description: AI can be your most powerful productivity tool… or a shortcut to cognitive decline. Neuroscientist Dr. Sahar Yousef and AI education company CEO Greg Shove debate the ultimate question: “AI feels like borrowing 30 IQ points, but will it ultimately make us dumber?” They will share new, unpublished UC Berkeley data showing that most “AI augmentation” is shallow cognitive offloading in disguise and outline a research-backed playbook for HOW you can leverage AI without losing what makes you most valuable: your mind.

events.name: Theory to Practice: Google’s Responsible Approach to AI
events.description: Every day, more of us interact with AI, prompting deeper questions about how these products are built. This workshop will allow participants to understand the intricacies of LLM training, providing a deeper grasp of how these models truly function. More critically, we'll dive into the essential strategies for mitigating risks and building safeguards into generative AI products. Participants will engage in activities to understand AI red teaming, using golden sets to calibrate AI responses to sensitive prompts, and discuss the potential and limits of various approaches.

events.name: Artificial Imagination and The Fiction After Cinema
events.description: This conference examines how AI, beyond cost reduction and visual effects, inaugurates a post-photographic rupture. Cinema's factuality—rooted in photographic capture of what was—confronts AI's counterfactuality: statistical generation of what could be, freed from indexical constraints. After metabolizing cinematic language, AI opens unprecedented fictional territories: evolving generative narratives, multiversal storytelling, dissolution of boundaries between creation and reception. We explore these new fictions that transcend filming constraints and classical narration, revealing how counterfactual generation catalyzes an anthropological mutation of our fictional regimes, and a means of resistance to the dissolution of common truth.

events.name: Mentor Session: Gulay Ozkan (GEDS Strategic Design Consultancy)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Blockchain & Cryptocurrency, Product Management/Development, Entrepreneurship.

events.name: Gulay Ozkan Book Signing
events.description: Gulay Ozkan will be signing copies of “The Age of Sameness: A Continuous Life Shaped by Human Creativity” at the SXSW Bookstore.

events.name: Something Feels Off: The Age of Sameness – Book Launch
events.description:
We go to the same cafés, follow the same glossy lists, and sit in front of the same Netflix queue. Increasingly, we are beginning to think in similar ways, shaped in part by AI tools. Many of us feel something is off, yet struggle to name it. It is not loud or dramatic. It is an ambient condition we live inside. Gulay Ozkan calls this sameness.
In this session, she introduces her book The Age of Sameness: A Continuous Experience Shaped by Human Creativity, traces how this condition has taken shape, and asks a deeper question: whether the conditions for human creativity still exist.

events.name: Featured Session: A Conversation With Spotify's New Co-CEO About The Past, Present And Future Of Delivering Creativity To The World
events.description: Twenty years ago, the music industry faced an existential crisis. Piracy was reshaping consumption, the old model was unraveling, and no clear path forward existed. Spotify was born from a simple but radical belief: that technology could reconnect artists and fans in a way that worked for both. Join Gustav for a look at the future of Spotify’s technological transformation across music, podcasts, and audiobooks, exploring how user control, creator innovation, and thoughtful technology are shaping the next chapter of entertainment.

events.name: The Storytelling Paradox: Reflect, Refract, or Revolutionize
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description: Storytellers reveal truths that connect us—uncovering hidden patterns, holding up mirrors to society, exploring our shared humanity, and amplifying voices at the margins. Individual stories gain power when they accumulate, from the #MeToo movement to documentaries that change how we see the world. But decades of human choices—about technology, distribution, and who profits -—have left storytellers without basic infrastructure to tell stories that matter. How do storytellers preserve their power to reveal, connect, and transform?

events.name: Humanoid Robots Are Clocking In: Meet Who’s Putting Them To Work
events.description: You’ve seen the viral humanoid demos—now meet the people actually shipping them. From hospitals to warehouses, humanoid robots are already on shift. They ride elevators, open doors, and work alongside people in busy environments. But making that leap from prototype to real-world, reliable performance? It’s hard. This panel brings together renowned founders and researchers who’ve built and deployed some of the first real humanoids at scale. Join us for a behind-the-scenes conversation about what's working, what’s not, and what it really takes to get robots out of the lab and into the world.

events.name: Can AI Learn Your Voice? Let's Find Out Live
events.description:
Can AI learn your unique voice? In this session, AI engineer & educator Harper Carroll (Stanford, Meta) fine-tunes an open-source model on her own X posts, and tests the result live.

While the model trains, she breaks down the science behind what's happening: how AI models are just probability machines, why smaller models can outperform larger ones for specific tasks (and what this means for you, your business, & your wallet), open vs. closed-source models, and how fine-tuning fundamentally changes a model's output compared to prompt engineering alone. This is for all levels of understanding.

events.name: Building the Next Generation of Trustworthy AI: From Generic Cloud Models to Custom, Local Intelligence
events.description: AI adoption is growing, but cloud-based one-size-fits-all models are hitting limits — high costs, privacy concerns, environmental damage, & latency issues. This session will explore why the next generation of AI will be specialized, expert, & local. Panelists will unpack how custom models, trained on proprietary data and deployed closer to the user, can deliver improved performance, privacy, & scalability with a more sustainable infrastructure. They'll share how this approach is reshaping businesses and privacy expectations — and what it takes to make it widespread.

name: Heather Collins, PhD
title: Cognitive Neuroscientist, Innovation curator, Peak Performance Expert

events.name: Couture Cognition: How Fashion Shapes Our Brains
events.description: What if fashion could rewire your brain? Neuroscientist Dr. Heather Collins discusses Neurostyle: How shoes and clothing activate memory, mood, and neural storytelling—boosting focus, emotional resonance, and curiosity. From color-triggered cognition to texture-based associations, fashion activates neural empathy through shared aesthetic choices. Discover how style shapes brain states and how professionals—from designers to technologists—can harness fashion’s cognitive power to craft more inclusive, immersive, and high-performing environments, from runways to boardrooms.

events.name: Brains & Bots: Evolving the Future of Intelligence Together
events.description: Does offloading thinking to AI cause cognitive atrophy or free our brains to think about more complex problems? That’s the million-neuron question. This provocative panel explores “adaptive intelligence”, where human and machine learning loops converge. A neuroscientist, futurist, and AI visionary unpack how co-learning systems, neuromorphic design, and spatial computing can amplify creativity, decision-making, and human agency in a world where algorithms don’t just support us, they shape us.

events.name: Featured Session: Multiplayer Futures: Co-Creating a Vision of SXSW 2030 (live!)
events.description:
What if the future isn't predicted by one speaker, but imagined by thousands? In this radically inclusive and participatory session, you won't just hear a keynote – you'll co-create one. 

Sketch your vision of SXSW 2030, and watch your idea become part of a map of the room's collective imagination and intelligence. Then we’ll explore its signals, themes and provocations, and leave you with a clearer lens to navigate the rest of the week. 

All live. All together.

events.name: Henry Coutinho-Mason Book Signing
events.description: Henry will be signing copies of “Future Normal” at the SXSW Bookstore.

events.name: Foresight Improv
events.description:
Do you wish you could predict the future? Come challenge our panel of futurists as they try to do it themselves! 

In 2024, SXSW attendees put a panel of futurists’ knowledge to the test, challenging them to improvise their view on the future of 18 audience-selected topics from toilet paper to war to thinking. Attendees submitted ideas into a fishbowl that spurred a constructive view of the future of a topic, in 3 minutes or less. This year we hope to return bigger and better with some new tricks and twists. Expect a rousing, dynamic, fun-filled adventure into the future and beyond!

events.name: AI Builders Meet Up
events.description: Join fellow founders, engineers, researchers, and product leaders who are building the next generation of AI products. This casual meetup brings together hands-on innovators to swap stories, share tools, and talk about what’s working (and what’s not) in the fast-moving world of AI. Whether you’re training models, shipping products, or scaling teams—come meet your peers and compare notes

events.name: Designing the Future of Human Performance
events.description:
What happens when elite athleticism meets cutting-edge technology? This session explores how human performance, innovation, and storytelling converge to push the boundaries of what’s possible in sport and beyond.

A conversation with Paralympian Hunter Woodhall, Olympic Gold medalist Tara Davis-Woodhall, Deloitte’s US Sports Chief Business Officer Anthony Stephan, and Narrative principal Justin Moore-Lewy.

events.name: Featured Session: How to Design a Company That AI Can’t Outpace
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description:
Yes. We’re failing at AI. But it’s not because the tools aren’t good enough. We’re failing because we’re designing for a system that’s already obsolete. The winners of the AI era won’t be the fastest tool adopters, but the ones who redesign how work is organized, value is created, and ecosystems are navigated.

In this session,, Ian shows you how to think and build like an AI-native and re-architect organizations, sharing a practical map and mindset shift to thrive in a future where what’s valued changes in real time.

events.name: Will Your Job Still Exist in Five Years? Redesigning Work in the Age of AI
events.description: In this 30-minute keynote, Michelle Schneider explores how artificial intelligence is reshaping not just jobs, but the very architecture of work. Moving beyond the simplistic narrative of “automation versus humans,” she examines how tasks are being reorganized, how expertise is being redefined, and what it truly means to design work in the age of intelligent systems.Drawing on two decades leading high-performing teams at global technology companies and her current work at the frontier of AI transformation, Michelle offers a clear and provocative framework for understanding what comes next — and how leaders can prepare.Following the keynote, Michelle will be joined by Ian Beacraft and journalist Cris Pelajo for a core conversation, expanding the dialogue and exploring practical, human-centered implications for organizations, workers, and society.

events.name: Securing Agency: Ownership in the Era of AI
events.description: As AI agents begin to act on our behalf—signing contracts, moving value, and managing data—we face a new crisis of authority: How does an autonomous system prove it truly represents you? Ian Rogers, Ledger CXO, explores the evolution of ownership and control in an AI-native world. This session moves beyond protecting assets to securing human intent. Discover why cryptographic signing and hardware-backed control are the essential layers for a responsible agent economy. Learn how to embrace the massive potential of AI without sacrificing autonomy or the human root of trust.

events.name: Thrive or Survive: Why Creativity is the Key to an AI-Future
events.description: AI may make life easier, but at what cost to our imagination? Like a bike we stop riding, our creativity weakens when we overrely on AI. Still, while adults may feel rusty, we can get back on. For children, the risk is even greater: if they never build that muscle in the first place—never learn to ride at all—there’s nothing to return to. This all comes just as jobs are being disrupted, problems grow more complex, and we need more imagination, not less. This panel explores how we can grow creatively with AI (versus be diminished by it) and what we must build now to make that future possible.

events.name: The Risks of Making a Film About NDAs
events.description:
The First Rule of NDAs - Don’t Talk About NDAs

How do you make a film about the weaponization of NDAs when people are prohibited from even admitting they signed one? This panel will examine how the film My NDA was made and how the filmmakers attempted to mitigate the risks faced by the film’s participants and the production itself. Panelists will discuss the thorny documentary ethics challenges that come into play when subjects are taking legal risks that could potentially lead to significant consequences.

events.name: The Storytelling Paradox: Reflect, Refract, or Revolutionize
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description: Storytellers reveal truths that connect us—uncovering hidden patterns, holding up mirrors to society, exploring our shared humanity, and amplifying voices at the margins. Individual stories gain power when they accumulate, from the #MeToo movement to documentaries that change how we see the world. But decades of human choices—about technology, distribution, and who profits -—have left storytellers without basic infrastructure to tell stories that matter. How do storytellers preserve their power to reveal, connect, and transform?

events.name: Space Technology and Innovation Conversation
events.description: Have an idea that could launch the space industry into its next era? Join a vibrant gathering of space tech pioneers, passionate enthusiasts, and established space professionals. This is your platform to pitch innovative concepts, receive feedback on new and expanding ideas, and forge powerful connections. Come to share, learn, and collaborate with the brightest minds charting the future of space.

events.name: Lights Out: Preserving Our Night Sky in the Modern Space Age
events.description: The increasing number and brightness of satellite constellations in Earth's orbit are significantly impacting ground-based and optical astronomy. So how do we balance humanity's expansion into space while protecting our study of the stars from Earth? Join the conversation on a sustainable future for space and hear from the experts on the front lines—astronomers, satellite companies, and space industry pioneers—as they discuss collaborative solutions to mitigate satellite brightness and preserve our night sky.

events.name: AI & the Brain: As We Embrace AI, Let’s Not Forget Our Minds
events.venue: The Westin Austin Downtown - Paramount I-II
events.description:
Across homes, offices, and classrooms, AI is becoming ubiquitous—mapping our routes, writing our code, even dreaming up art in seconds. What once sounded like science fiction is now silently transforming our daily existence. And yet, amid this great digital leap forward, a quieter, more fragile system is at stake: the human brain.
We are perched at a precipice in history. As AI systems grow in power and presence, we must ask a question too few dare to voice: What is this doing to us - our capacity to explore, to learn, to reason, and to create? The machines are learning. But are we forgetting?

name: J.P. Guiseppi
title: Group Creative Lead at Google Strategic Content Partnerships

events.name: XR Immersive Filmmaking & The Future of Entertainment
events.description: XR is not just a new format—it’s a new language of interactive cinematic storytelling. Join director Doug Liman’s award-winning team 30 Ninjas as they explore how working with Google XR teams they are combining emerging formats like volumetric capture, spatial narrative, 3D engines and real-time Google LLM driven characters are reshaping entertainment from the inside out. Learn how cinematic craft, game mechanics, XR and AI are merging at the highest level to create the future of audience experience and participation—where the viewer isn’t just watching the story, they’re inside it.

events.name: Intelligence on Tap: Designing the Frontier Firm
events.description: Welcome to the era of intelligence on demand. Microsoft’s Work Trend Index introduces the Frontier Firm: built on abundant AI, where digital agents aren’t tools, they’re teammates. While some fear AI replacing jobs, the real risk is ignoring it. With 80% of employees maxed out and 82% of leaders betting on AI, the question isn’t if you’ll use digital labor, it’s how. Frontier Firms are rethinking org charts, hiring AI trainers, and managing human-agent ratios like headcount. This is your blueprint for building a business where AI scales work and where human judgment stays at the center.

name: Jacqueline Corbelli
title: CEO and Founder of BrightLine / Author of Changemaker: A Modern Playbook

events.name: Harnessing AI Without Losing Our Minds: A Playbook
events.description: As AI reshapes everything, how do you pivot your life, career, or company before you’re left behind? Join Jacqueline Corbelli—3x founder and investor, UN and Vatican advisor, and author of Changemaker—for a practical session on navigating inflection points. Drawing on decades of experience transforming companies, Corbelli shares proven solutions to empower individuals and companies, offering a modern framework for using AI, adopting the right mindset, and evolving in a world where five-year plans no longer apply. Are we too early, too late—or right on time?

name: Jacqueline O'Reilly
title: Professor at the University of Sussex Business School & Co-Director of the ESRC Digital Futures at Work Research Centre (Digit)

events.name: Glowing Brains & Robosuits: Terminating the AI Cliché
events.description:
The metaphors we use shape the futures we create. While the whole world becomes human-AI hybrid, we still talk about AI like a god, ghost or omnipotent spreadsheet.
Corporate ‘cyborg-centaur’ sci-fi clichés trap organisations in macho, mechanistic tropes. We urgently need new myths to think with. Bring your sledgehammer as we deconstruct the lazy visual language of AI. Whether you’re designing tech, making art or building teams, the models unveiled in this session will give you the tools you need to take control and make AI an effective, equitable partner in the redesign of the new workplace.

events.name: Mentor Session: Jamie Wood (Autonomic)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Business Development, Future of Work, Entrepreneurship.

events.name: Seeing Is Believing: The Vision Behind Humanoid & Robotic AI
events.description: Humanoids and robots are moving from science fiction to reality, and vision is key to that evolution. Just as our visual cortex allows humans to perceive and adapt, machine vision gives robots the ability to understand, navigate, and interact with the world around them. This session explores how advances in depth sensing, AI compute, and cloud-scale learning are giving humanoids and robots sight, safety, and awareness to operate naturally among us—and what that means for the future of human-robot interaction.

events.name: Real-world Immersion at Scale: the Future of Spatial
events.description: Dive into the future of real‑world immersion as we explore how XR transforms theaters, museums, retail, and enterprise spaces into living digital playgrounds. This session dives into spatial computing, mixed reality, interactive environments, and AR wearables, revealing how creators reshape physical spaces into dynamic, programmable worlds. Experience how XR blurs physical and digital boundaries and stay for live demos showcasing these innovations. These hands‑on demonstrations will highlight how immersive technologies can transform familiar locations into unforgettable shared adventures.

events.name: Digital Analytics & Innovation Meet Up
events.description: Back by popular demand, this SXSW meetup reunites the data-obsessed and innovation-hungry. In a digital landscape that shifts daily, "analytics" is no longer just about reporting on what happened yesterday—it’s about architecting what happens tomorrow. As the gap between raw data and creative innovation narrows, the role of the analyst is evolving from observer to navigator. Whether you’re a digital analyst, a data scientist or a CMO, join us to move beyond standard KPIs and discuss advanced analytics in product innovation. Come swap war stories, debate ethics, and explore how data democratization is changing the creative process.

name: Jari Safi
title: VP Of AI and Computer Vision

events.name: Seeing Is Believing: The Vision Behind Humanoid & Robotic AI
events.description: Humanoids and robots are moving from science fiction to reality, and vision is key to that evolution. Just as our visual cortex allows humans to perceive and adapt, machine vision gives robots the ability to understand, navigate, and interact with the world around them. This session explores how advances in depth sensing, AI compute, and cloud-scale learning are giving humanoids and robots sight, safety, and awareness to operate naturally among us—and what that means for the future of human-robot interaction.

events.name: Featured Session: Launch, Land, Orbit: The Future of Space with Firefly
events.description: A fireside chat with Jason Kim, CEO, and Brigette Oakes, VP of Engineering, at Firefly Aerospace. In 2025, Firefly became the first commercial company to successfully land on the Moon, and now the company is working towards a repeat in 2026. Firefly’s next lunar is bigger and bolder, set to land on the far side of the Moon for the first time in American history. This company is simultaneously launching rockets to fill a void in the growing satellite market while also preparing to orbit a constellation of spacecraft in support of in-space maneuverability, space domain awareness, and more. Jason and Brigette will discuss what’s next for the company and where the space industry is headed next.

events.name: Accelerating Discovery: The Role of Agentic AI in Science
events.description: The new generation of AI models and agentic AI systems are fundamentally transforming the pace of scientific discovery, offering new ways to solve humanity's most complex challenges. By supercharging productivity and augmenting human expertise, these tools help researchers push the boundaries of knowledge faster than ever before. From synthesizing vast scientific literature and generating novel hypotheses to creating new research plans and executing simulations, agentic AI acts as a powerful collaborator in the lab. We are entering a new era of collaborative science, one where AI works alongside researchers to turn the next iteration into the next great breakthrough.

events.name: XR Immersive Filmmaking & The Future of Entertainment
events.description: XR is not just a new format—it’s a new language of interactive cinematic storytelling. Join director Doug Liman’s award-winning team 30 Ninjas as they explore how working with Google XR teams they are combining emerging formats like volumetric capture, spatial narrative, 3D engines and real-time Google LLM driven characters are reshaping entertainment from the inside out. Learn how cinematic craft, game mechanics, XR and AI are merging at the highest level to create the future of audience experience and participation—where the viewer isn’t just watching the story, they’re inside it.

events.name: Humanoid Robots Are Clocking In: Meet Who’s Putting Them To Work
events.description: You’ve seen the viral humanoid demos—now meet the people actually shipping them. From hospitals to warehouses, humanoid robots are already on shift. They ride elevators, open doors, and work alongside people in busy environments. But making that leap from prototype to real-world, reliable performance? It’s hard. This panel brings together renowned founders and researchers who’ve built and deployed some of the first real humanoids at scale. Join us for a behind-the-scenes conversation about what's working, what’s not, and what it really takes to get robots out of the lab and into the world.

events.name: Leading the AI Future: How to Think Exponentially in a Chaotic Decade
events.description: AI is accelerating markets, work, and entire industries faster than most leaders can process. In this Core Conversation, Kian Gohar (former Singularity University Executive Director) joins Jeff Duncan, CEO of Ingenuity Live, to explore how leaders must shift from linear thinking to exponential thinking. Drawing on experience with NASA, XPRIZE, and Fortune 500 companies, Kian shares mental models for navigating uncertainty, reframing risk and opportunity, and building a “future operating system” to lead confidently in an AI-driven decade.

events.name: From Burnout to Bandwidth: Using AI to Reclaim Your Day
events.description: Drowning in emails, planning, paperwork, and those boring (but necessary) administrative tasks? In this hands-on session, discover practical AI tools that automate routine tasks, generate content, analyze data, and simplify communication, freeing you to focus on students and strategy. Leave with real-world examples, ready-to-use ideas, and strategies to reduce burnout, improve efficiency, and reignite your creativity. Learn how AI can help you save time on low-level tasks so you can focus on the high-level tasks that bring maximum impact to your job.

events.name: Another ‘Disgruntled Employee,’ They Said
events.description: When WhatsApp’s former Head of Security raised alarms about flaws that put billions at risk, Meta fired him. He’s not the only insider punished for protecting the public. This session explores the cost of speaking up inside Big Tech—and how we can build real safeguards, legal protections, and public support so that truth-telling in the age of AI isn’t a career-ending act of courage.

events.name: GenMedia 101: Unlocking Creativity with AI & Google DeepMind
events.description: A hands-on session for artists, storytellers, and makers of all kinds. Build visuals, music, and media experiences - no code required. Every creative idea starts with a feeling: a scene in your mind, a sound in your head, or a story you keep coming back to. This workshop is for anyone who wants to bring those ideas to life, with a little help from generative AI. You’ll explore how tools like Veo (for video), Lyria (for music), and Nano Banana (for images) can help you shape what you imagine into something you can actually see, hear, and share. Laptop & headphones/earbuds recommended.

events.name: From Burnout to Bandwidth: Using AI to Reclaim Your Day
events.description: Drowning in emails, planning, paperwork, and those boring (but necessary) administrative tasks? In this hands-on session, discover practical AI tools that automate routine tasks, generate content, analyze data, and simplify communication, freeing you to focus on students and strategy. Leave with real-world examples, ready-to-use ideas, and strategies to reduce burnout, improve efficiency, and reignite your creativity. Learn how AI can help you save time on low-level tasks so you can focus on the high-level tasks that bring maximum impact to your job.

events.name: Featured Session: The Innovation Engine You’ve Never Heard of: Your Neighbor
events.description: Traditional corporate innovation keeps ideas locked away until launch, but GE Appliances innovation arm, FirstBuild, has flipped the script—inviting passionate enthusiasts to co-create products from day one. The result is a 93% failure rate that just so happens to drive million-dollar successes like the Opal Nugget Ice Maker, which raised $2.7 million in crowdfunding after the community helped shape its development. This session will explore how major corporations can harness the power of co-creation, where rough transparency builds more trust than polished presentations, and where your best product ideas might come from a Reddit user with a 3D printer. Learn how FirstBuild proves that building in public, failing fast, and letting communities vote with their wallets creates better products than closed-door development—and why the future of innovation looks less like a pipeline and more like a playground.

events.name: Featured Session: The Future of News
events.description: A thriving news industry is essential—not only to democracy, but also as a powerful platform for marketers and advertisers. Yet concerns about brand safety have caused many to retreat from news content, often based on outdated fears and misinformation. In an era of rapid technological disruption—including the rise of AI and increasing cultural polarization— this panel unites marketers and media experts to challenge assumptions and champion news. We'll explore how advertisers can re-engage with journalism in ways that uphold brand values, build consumer trust, and deliver real business results.

events.name: The Future of Work Is Still Human
events.description: As AI reshapes how we work, fundamental questions about voice and individual expression are emerging. Rahul Vohra, Founder of Superhuman Mail, explores why the most powerful tools don’t replace human judgment or flatten individual expression, but expand what people are capable of doing. Grounded in his experience building Superhuman Mail, Rahul discusses how founders and builders can design technology that amplifies human capability while ensuring the work still sounds, feels, and belongs to the user.

events.name: Game On: Reimagining Career Exploration Through Play
events.description: Manufacturing is high-tech, high-paying, and full of opportunity—yet this vital industry still ranks last among teens’ career choices. By blending game-based learning, Dimensions of Success, and authentic career experiences shaped by SMEs and teachers, a futuristic learning pod is reshaping how students view advanced manufacturing. After one semester, students are 75% more likely to consider careers in the field, and 63% want further learning. Discover how a global industry leader, a nonprofit, and educators are sparking new enthusiasm and transforming perceptions of technical careers.

events.name: The New Lab Partner: AI and Future Scientific Discovery
events.description: AI is quickly becoming an active thinking partner, completely changing scientific discovery. That means, potentially faster cures, new drug uses, and insights to combat viruses. But it sparks key philosophical questions: Who owns AI-generated insight? How do we validate it? Are we more innovative or dependent? Join experts to explore AI's role in surfacing new questions, defining co-discovery, and navigating its risks/rewards for human health. A critical conversation on AI's radical impact on discovery.

events.name: Mentor Session: Ji-Sook Yim (Alight Solutions)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Product Management/Development, Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning.

name: Joana Lenkova
title: Director of Cultural and Futures Intelligence

events.name: The LEGO Futures Imaginarium: Play Your Way to Tomorrow
events.description: What if your most imaginative future was already inside you, hidden in your most cherished moment of childhood play? The LEGO Imaginarium is a hands-on foresight workshop where participants use LEGO bricks, storytelling, and memory prompts to reconnect with their inner child and build physical models of possible futures. Guided by futures thinking tools, the LEGO system in play, and inspired by the creativity of kids, this session blends strategic foresight with playful, hands-on exploration to unlock fresh perspectives on what’s next in the future.

events.name: The Companies Building and Automating Space's Superhighway
events.description: How do we keep space sustainable as it gets more crowded? Enter the Space Superhighway: a network of in-space robots that will keep spacecraft fueled, repaired, upgraded and built in orbit. Just like highways rely on gas stations, tow trucks and repair shops, space will need support systems to be accessible for future generations. This tech is real TODAY. Our panel convenes accomplished doers from startups working to extend the life of satellites, reduce space debris and build a future where space is safe, scalable and sustainable for exploration and economic development to benefit society.

events.name: Featured Session: A Conversation with Andrew Ross Sorkin on the 1929 Financial Crash —  and Today’s Economy
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description:
Are we living through a 1929 moment and don't know it yet?

Global markets are navigating one of the most consequential inflection points in decades. Artificial intelligence is rewriting the rules of entire industries. Regulatory policies are shifting under our feet. Geopolitical tensions are rising. And through it all, a familiar pattern is emerging — one that Andrew Ross Sorkin has spent years studying.

Sorkin is the author of 1929: Inside the Greatest Crash in Wall Street History — and How It Shattered a Nation, an instant New York Times bestseller that reveals how overconfidence, speculation, and political dysfunction converged to produce the worst financial disasters in American history. He's also the co-anchor of CNBC's Squawk Box, founder of The New York Times' DealBook, and one of the most influential voices in finance and media.

In conversation with Joe Weisenthal — co-host of Bloomberg's cult-favorite Odd Lots podcast — Sorkin will draw the lines between 1929 and now: the warning signs that were missed then, the ones flashing today, and what it would take to avoid repeating history.

name: John Canning
title: Director of Developer Relations, Creators/CTO of Wonderseed Foundation

events.name: Mentor Session: John Canning (AMD/Wonderseed Foundation)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Content Strategy/Production, Immersive Technology.

events.name: Powerful AI, Terrible UX:  Designing a Smarter UX
events.description: Enterprise AI is getting more powerful, but for many users it still feels slow, awkward, and hard to trust. The problem is rarely the model or the data. It is the experience. Too many tools rely on chat interfaces that add cognitive load, cause prompt fatigue, and fail in high pressure workflows. This session explores why enterprise AI UX keeps breaking down and how cognitive design can reduce friction, rebuild trust, and make AI fit how people actually work.

name: John Hagel
title: Founder & CEO
company: Beyond Our Edge & Center For Growth

events.name: John Hagel Book Signing
events.description: John Hagel will be signing copies of “The Journey Beyond Fear” at the SXSW Bookstore.

events.name: Creative Destruction Unleashed by Artificial Intelligence
events.description: Artificial intelligence is a highly disruptive technology that will challenge existing institutions in all industries and domains globally. Traditional approaches to creating value will become less and less effective as this technology spreads. On the other side, this technology opens up new potential to create exponentially expanding value but, to harness this potential, we need to become very creative in imagining and implementing new approaches to managing organizations.

name: John Palfrey
company: John D. and Catherine T. MacArthur Foundation

events.name: Featured Session: Reclaiming our Humanity in the Age of AI
events.description: Too often, AI feels like something that’s done to us, not with us or for us. It will affect every aspect of our lives. But AI is neither poison nor panacea. It is and will be what we make it. Now is the moment to shape the future of AI for people. This panel will explore how we build the ecosystem and infrastructure to ensure that people will benefit the most from AI. Experts in AI, philanthropy, and advocacy, will offer a new way of thinking about AI that moves from Silicon Valley’s mantra of “move fast and break things” to an alternative vision that centers our humanity.

events.name: The End of Work as We Know It: AI, Humans & the New Global Power Dynamics
events.description:
Work is not disappearing.
It is being redistributed — between humans, machines, and the systems that connect them.
As humans become augmented by data and algorithms, the architecture of work — jobs, skills, organizations, and even nations — is being rewritten.
This session explores how AI infrastructure, talent ecosystems, and strategic policy are redefining global power dynamics. 
If work as we knew it is over,
who controls what comes next?  Do we need controls?
And how should leaders, workers, and societies prepare for this shift?

events.name: Featured Session: Enough with the Bullsh*T: BuzzFeed Founder Shares His Plan to Make the Internet Fun Again
events.description: After years of doomscrolling and algorithmic slop, BuzzFeed founder Jonah Peretti believes the internet is overdue for a reset, and for the first time he will share his plan to make the internet fun again. We’ll take you behind the scenes of BuzzFeed’s AI incubator where we are creating a new medium for content, self-expression, and social connection. We’ll start with big picture ideas you can apply to your business. Then you’ll see live demos of new products and attendees will get special access to the latest from our lab. You’ll see how AI-powered experiences can create shared cultural moments and help people explore the world together, creating a better alternative to filter bubbles and doomscrolling. We are transforming our business with a new approach to AI, and you can join us to think about how to transform your own work to maximize creativity, human connection, and growth.

events.name: Humanoid Robots Are Clocking In: Meet Who’s Putting Them To Work
events.description: You’ve seen the viral humanoid demos—now meet the people actually shipping them. From hospitals to warehouses, humanoid robots are already on shift. They ride elevators, open doors, and work alongside people in busy environments. But making that leap from prototype to real-world, reliable performance? It’s hard. This panel brings together renowned founders and researchers who’ve built and deployed some of the first real humanoids at scale. Join us for a behind-the-scenes conversation about what's working, what’s not, and what it really takes to get robots out of the lab and into the world.

events.name: The Systems We Build: Business Models for Meaningful XR
events.description: XR is booming… Or is it? How secure are business models in the interactive/immersive sector and how well do they support creators to have social impact? And how do you thrive - not just survive - as a creator, producer, developer of this type of work, right now? Acknowledging challenges and uncertainty but also highlighting opportunities, the panel reflect on their own journeys - including successes, challenges and startings-over - to explore alternative models and offer practical advice for creators and producers to make work that matters: including protecting your IP and your wellbeing.

events.name: XR Experience Spotlight
XR Experience Spotlight
XR Experience Spotlight
Portraits Beyond the Frame: Jonathan Yeo & Spectacles
events.description: Jonathan and Ben Wymer, Senior Director of Global Brand Experience at Snap Inc., will explore how artists are experimenting with immersive technologies and smart glasses; the story behind his striking red portrait of King Charles and its unexpected cultural impact; and what his groundbreaking AR collaboration with Spectacles, premiering in the US at SXSW, reveals about how we might experience art in museums through wearable devices and new digital layers in the years ahead.

events.name: Spectacular: The Art of Jonathan Yeo in Augmented Reality

name: Jordan Kolosey
title: VP, Business Strategy, Innovation & Data Operations

events.name: OpenAI & Spurs: Building Human-Centered AI for Fans, Partners, and Communities
events.description: How do you scale AI without losing the human touch? In this session, leaders from the San Antonio Spurs and OpenAI share how they’re using AI to create more meaningful experiences for fans, partners, and communities. From personalized fan engagement and family-focused experiences to internal productivity and decision-making, the Spurs offer a real-world look at how purpose-built AI tools are being used on and off the court. Attendees will walk away with practical lessons on responsible AI adoption, concrete use cases from a global sports franchise, and a blueprint for putting people first while bringing cutting-edge technology to the courtside.

events.name: Operating Systems of the 3rd Millennium: AI + XR + NEURO
events.description: Neural interfaces are entering a new phase where AI algorithms read brain signals in real-time and power adaptive feedback loops. This panel convenes OpenBCI and cross-disciplinary experts to show how AI turns raw brain signals into actionable insights, powers real-time closed-loop haptics and visuals, and scales from lab demos to consumer and research-grade neurotech. We’ll dissect technical hurdles, safety imperatives, and the roadmap to trustworthy neurotechnology.

name: Julia Kuchman
title: Vice President, Stablecoin Product, Commercial & Money Movement Solutions

events.name: See Where the Money Goes: Rebuild Trust in Humanitarian Aid
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description:
Trust is under pressure across industries, but in the humanitarian sector it has become existential. As public skepticism around waste, corruption, and opaque systems grows, support for aid can disappear fast—as seen in 2025 with sweeping cuts to U.S. foreign assistance driven by accountability concerns.

This session explores how blockchain is reshaping humanitarian aid by enabling real-time, verifiable tracking of stablecoin-based funds from donor to recipient. We’ll examine what trust-by-design systems mean not just for aid, but for any sector grappling with accountability at scale.

events.name: Building AI Pathways for HBCU Talent & Community Impact
events.venue: Austin Marriott Downtown - Waller Ballroom D
events.description: As AI rapidly reshapes education and work, HBCUs sit at a critical intersection of talent development, cultural stewardship, and community accountability. This panel brings together higher education, industry, and ecosystem leaders to examine how ethical AI pathways can be built with HBCUs—not around them. Panelists will explore practical models for aligning curriculum, workforce pipelines, industry partnerships, and community impact so AI innovation leads to access, ownership, and long-term opportunity for Black talent and communities.

name: Julissa Germosen
title: Tech Executive, Author, Human Connection & AI Leadership Expert

events.name: Mentor Session: Julissa Germosén
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Business Development, Storytelling.

events.name: Brains & Bots: Evolving the Future of Intelligence Together
events.description: Does offloading thinking to AI cause cognitive atrophy or free our brains to think about more complex problems? That’s the million-neuron question. This provocative panel explores “adaptive intelligence”, where human and machine learning loops converge. A neuroscientist, futurist, and AI visionary unpack how co-learning systems, neuromorphic design, and spatial computing can amplify creativity, decision-making, and human agency in a world where algorithms don’t just support us, they shape us.

name: Justin Haan
title: Partner and Chair of Technology Transactions Group

events.name: AI Infrastructure: Building Stargate & Beyond
events.description:
The AI revolution has changed how we work, play, and more. It is also driving a major shift in the backend infrastructure that powers these developments.

As AI capabilities accelerate, leading tech companies are moving beyond software and into the physical world of data centers, power, and infrastructure. Why? Because the future of intelligence now depends as much on electricity and silicon as it does on algorithms.

In this session, OpenAI pulls back the curtain on what it really takes to scale AI today. The conversation explores how data center development has become mission-critical, how decisions about where to build are made, and what happens when AI growth collides with real-world limits such as energy, land, and supply chains.

From energy partnerships to site selection, this conversation will reveal how data center development has shaped the landscape in nearby locations – such as OpenAI’s Stargate facility in Abilene, Texas – and perhaps someday far away in Earth’s orbit.

events.name: Designing the Future of Human Performance
events.description:
What happens when elite athleticism meets cutting-edge technology? This session explores how human performance, innovation, and storytelling converge to push the boundaries of what’s possible in sport and beyond.

A conversation with Paralympian Hunter Woodhall, Olympic Gold medalist Tara Davis-Woodhall, Deloitte’s US Sports Chief Business Officer Anthony Stephan, and Narrative principal Justin Moore-Lewy.

events.name: The Future is Low-Tech: Lessons from the Early 2000s
events.venue: Austin Marriott Downtown - Waller Ballroom B
events.description:
Let's rewind back to the early 2000s – slower interfaces, finite gameplay, loading bars, shared family computers and digital experiences that rewarded patience, curiosity and real-world interaction.

In this talk, we'll unpack the overlooked design features of the era and explore how they can be applied today.

This session is for designers, educators, technologists and decision-makers who want to take part in building a low-tech future!

events.name: Winning with AI: From the Masters to Wimbledon and more
events.description:
What happens when AI meets the world’s most iconic sporting events?

Join IBM for a future-focused conversation on powering the fan experience with generative AI — led by peeling back the curtain on the world-class technology and data operation tradition of the Masters, which begins in a few short weeks.

The conversation will also explore how AI innovation is fueling the racing precision of Scuderia Ferrari, the legendary stage of Wimbledon and more.

From crafting smarter operations to boosting hyper-personalized real-time engagement, learn how technology is redefining sports in real time.

events.name: Electrification: Moving the Needle at Both Ends of the Scale
events.description: Electric vehicles are often discussed as a climate solution or a technological milestone, but their most powerful benefits may be human. Beyond emissions, EVs have the potential to change how our cities sound and feel, how people experience daily movement, and how transportation impacts health, stress, and overall quality of life. In this panel, ALSO President and Co-Founder Chris Yu and Rivian Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe explore the science behind why EV adoption can meaningfully improve everyday life, why EV adoption leads to better public health and more time spent outdoors, and the broader systems required to make electric mobility feel intuitive, enjoyable, and worth choosing every day.

events.name: On with Kara Swisher Live
events.description: Join award-winning journalist Kara Swisher for a live taping of On with Kara Swisher, where she’ll sit down with a special guest for a no-holds-barred interview.

events.name: Pivot Live
events.description: Join tech journalist Kara Swisher and NYU Professor Scott Galloway as they offer sharp, unfiltered insights into the biggest stories in tech, business, and politics. They’ll make bold predictions, pick winners and losers, and bicker and banter like no one else. After all, with great power comes great scrutiny.

events.name: Featured Session: Reclaiming our Humanity in the Age of AI
events.description: Too often, AI feels like something that’s done to us, not with us or for us. It will affect every aspect of our lives. But AI is neither poison nor panacea. It is and will be what we make it. Now is the moment to shape the future of AI for people. This panel will explore how we build the ecosystem and infrastructure to ensure that people will benefit the most from AI. Experts in AI, philanthropy, and advocacy, will offer a new way of thinking about AI that moves from Silicon Valley’s mantra of “move fast and break things” to an alternative vision that centers our humanity.

events.name: Building Reality: Generative AI for the Physical World
events.description: Generative AI has rapidly advanced in text, imagery, and 3D content, but its potential to shape the physical world remains largely untapped. This panel explores how AI can be used to generate designs for things that can be built, manufactured, or constructed, spanning products, machines, buildings, and infrastructure. Experts in computational design, AI research, and digital fabrication will discuss current challenges, technical hurdles, and the foundational work needed to unlock future impact and bring generative design into the realm of the physical.

name: Kartik Hosanagar
title: John C. Hower Professor of Operations, Information and Decisions; Co-Director, Wharton Human-AI Research
company: The Wharton School at the University of Pennsylvania

events.name: Mentor Session: Kartik Hosanagar (The Wharton School/University of Pennsylvania)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Entrepreneurship.

events.name: How to Build AI-First Products: Models, Memory, Mastery
events.description: AI is reshaping how we build software products—from conversational UX to fast-evolving AI models that can upend product roadmaps and even entire products. In this session, a leading product exec and a Wharton AI expert share how to design products powered by large language models. You'll learn how AI is reshaping product roles, what it takes to build with ever-evolving models, and why great UX now means thinking in conversations, not clicks.

name: Kary Bheemaiah
title: Chief Tech & Innovation Officer- Capgemini Invent I Exec Fellow – World Economic Forum

events.name: Technology Convergence: Identifying the Advantage
events.description: History's biggest technology waves, from electrification to the internet, were convergence stories, derived not from single inventions but a combination of them. Today, that pattern repeats where AI, robotics, advanced materials, bioengineering, and energy systems are producing solutions that no single discipline, company, or lab could create alone. Based on the World Economic Forum's Technology Convergence Report, this session unveils the 3C Framework - Combination, Convergence, Compounding - analyzing 246 technologies across 8 advanced technology domains, spotlighting pioneers excelling from this wave of fusion plays. See how convergence unlocks new opportunities and learn to spot, and activate, the next wave before it breaks.

events.name: Technology Convergence: Winning the Exponential Upswing
events.description: Every industrial revolution started with mashups. Today, advanced digital, biological, and physical technologies are creating their S-Curves together, unleashing a burst of convergence that generates value none could release alone, a dynamic mapped by the World Economic Forum's Technology Convergence Report. In this hands-on session, executives and founders will work through a diagnostic framework to map the intricacies of today's technology landscape, stress-test where their next major bet sits within the convergence wave, and walk away with a practical lens they can apply immediately.

name: Kasley Killam
title: Author
company: The Art and Science of Connection

events.name: The Great Recalibration: Centering Humanity in the Age of AI
events.description:
AI is rapidly reshaping how we work, create, and connect—yet at the same time, people are craving the tactile, the real, and the human. As AI-generated content floods our world, a new question emerges: where do we fit?

This panel explores the tension between acceleration and authenticity. How do we design AI that strengthens community instead of replacing it? How do we protect meaning, creativity, and trust in an era of misinformation and automation?

Innovation leaders will examine the cultural shift already underway and share a vision for an AI-powered future where human values and relationships remain at the center.

events.name: Featured Session: Social Health Trends & Predictions: Connection is the New Frontier
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description: Social health is today where mental health was 10-15 years ago—but we’ve reached a turning point. That was the bold claim from leading expert Kasley Killam in her SXSW 2025 opening keynote. This year, she’s back to reveal the most important breakthroughs, trends, and predictions shaping the rapidly growing movement to transform society through connection and community. Drawing on new research and cutting-edge innovation around the globe, Killam will surface surprising insights, the most promising opportunities for impact, and an inspiring vision of what’s next. No matter your industry, this is the session you can’t miss if you want to understand—and help lead—the future of relationships, longevity, and human thriving.

events.name: Kasley Killam Book Signing
events.description: Kasley Killam will be signing copies of “Art and Science of Connection” at the SXSW Bookstore.

events.name: Mentor Session: Katy Rudd (Salesforce)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Future of Work.

events.name: AI Infrastructure: Building Stargate & Beyond
events.description:
The AI revolution has changed how we work, play, and more. It is also driving a major shift in the backend infrastructure that powers these developments.

As AI capabilities accelerate, leading tech companies are moving beyond software and into the physical world of data centers, power, and infrastructure. Why? Because the future of intelligence now depends as much on electricity and silicon as it does on algorithms.

In this session, OpenAI pulls back the curtain on what it really takes to scale AI today. The conversation explores how data center development has become mission-critical, how decisions about where to build are made, and what happens when AI growth collides with real-world limits such as energy, land, and supply chains.

From energy partnerships to site selection, this conversation will reveal how data center development has shaped the landscape in nearby locations – such as OpenAI’s Stargate facility in Abilene, Texas – and perhaps someday far away in Earth’s orbit.

events.name: Unleashing Students' Tech Genius to Ensure Mental Wellness
events.description: Panelists will discuss emerging tech-driven mental health solutions and the importance of involving end users – in this case, college students – in their development. Centered on the student-led creation of a first-of-its-kind AI tool to support mental health, this discussion with the project’s researchers and students will emphasize the need to let research guide how we integrate AI into healthcare to harness its benefits without creating new harms, all while empowering students to become ethical stewards of AI.

events.name: Leading the AI Future: How to Think Exponentially in a Chaotic Decade
events.description: AI is accelerating markets, work, and entire industries faster than most leaders can process. In this Core Conversation, Kian Gohar (former Singularity University Executive Director) joins Jeff Duncan, CEO of Ingenuity Live, to explore how leaders must shift from linear thinking to exponential thinking. Drawing on experience with NASA, XPRIZE, and Fortune 500 companies, Kian shares mental models for navigating uncertainty, reframing risk and opportunity, and building a “future operating system” to lead confidently in an AI-driven decade.

events.name: The Companies Building and Automating Space's Superhighway
events.description: How do we keep space sustainable as it gets more crowded? Enter the Space Superhighway: a network of in-space robots that will keep spacecraft fueled, repaired, upgraded and built in orbit. Just like highways rely on gas stations, tow trucks and repair shops, space will need support systems to be accessible for future generations. This tech is real TODAY. Our panel convenes accomplished doers from startups working to extend the life of satellites, reduce space debris and build a future where space is safe, scalable and sustainable for exploration and economic development to benefit society.

events.name: Event Tech Meet Up
events.description:
Are you passionate about the intersection of technology and events? Whether you're at the forefront of managing event technologies, actively involved in producing software for event management, or simply intrigued by the innovative world of event tech, this Meet Up is for you.

What to Expect:
-Networking Opportunities
-Industry Insights
-Collaboration Opportunities

Don't miss this opportunity to expand your network, gain valuable insights, and be a part of shaping the future of event technology.

events.name: Meet Your New Work Team: AI, Chatbots, and the Workplace
events.description: "Hello, how can I help you?" Standard questions that once started human conversations are now chatbot prompts. As AI embeds in workplaces worldwide, how are these tools transforming professional interactions and workplace culture? What risks are encoded in systems mediating communication between colleagues and customers? This panel examines the EU's framework for compliant AI deployment, emerging hiring trends, and shifting skill demands. From voice interfaces to decision-making algorithms, discover how workplace AI is being designed-and what it means for workers, employers, and society.

events.name: The Great Recalibration: Centering Humanity in the Age of AI
events.description:
AI is rapidly reshaping how we work, create, and connect—yet at the same time, people are craving the tactile, the real, and the human. As AI-generated content floods our world, a new question emerges: where do we fit?

This panel explores the tension between acceleration and authenticity. How do we design AI that strengthens community instead of replacing it? How do we protect meaning, creativity, and trust in an era of misinformation and automation?

Innovation leaders will examine the cultural shift already underway and share a vision for an AI-powered future where human values and relationships remain at the center.

events.name: From Policy to Practice: AI Implementation in Government
events.description: Artificial intelligence is no longer theoretical. Across the country, governments are moving from conversations about AI governance to real-world deployment — modernizing services, strengthening cybersecurity, improving legislative analysis, and rethinking how the public sector operates. But implementation is where the real work begins.

events.name: Mentor Session: Laurel Prime (Google)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Entrepreneurship, Product Management/Development.

name: Lawrence Lessig
title: Roy L. Furman Professor of Law and Leadership

events.name: The Knowledge Paradox: When Does Sharing Become Theft?
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description: For decades, the open movement championed sharing as the path to knowledge equity—billions of works freely accessible and reusable across research, education, and culture. But AI weaponizes that openness: what was built to democratize knowledge now trains models that concentrate power and profit. Open data scraping. Closed model development. Free content fueling extraction. As we mark Creative Commons' 25th anniversary, we confront an impossible question: how do we keep knowledge open without enabling its exploitation?

events.name: The Writers Room LIVE
events.description: A behind-the-scenes look inside a real comedy writers’ room with Seth Green, his Robot Chicken co-creator, and his writing team. Watch as they break stories, chase punch lines, and riff in real time onstage, using Superhuman as a creative collaborator.

events.name: Space Workforce Meet Up
events.description: Texas has always been the frontier of America’s biggest ideas - from cattle drives to microchips, music revolutions to moonshots. In 2026, the frontier is shifting upward, and the call for the New Space Cowboy is here.  In this era of commercial space, the next giant leap won’t be driven by astronauts, but welders, coders, machinists, technicians, scientists, mission operators, and dreamers. And no place on Earth is better positioned to cultivate this next gen of talent. Join the Space Workforce Incubator for Texas for a meetup that blends the spirit of the Wild West with the future's promise.

events.name: Featured Session: The Internet of Value Meets the Internet of Intelligence
events.description:
As AI and blockchain mature in parallel, we are witnessing the emergence of two powerful networks: the Internet of Intelligence, where autonomous AI systems reason and act, and the Internet of Value, where tokenized assets and stablecoins move frictionlessly across borders.

This session explores what happens when these two networks converge — when AI agents can hold, send, and earn value — and how this convergence will reshape global commerce, financial systems, and enterprise operations. Drawing from her experience leading AI transformation at Circle, an internet infrastructure company pioneering stablecoins, Li Fan will share both the vision and operational realities of building compliant, intelligent, and scalable financial infrastructure for the AI era.
