events.name: The Consumer’s Guide to Beating the Bots
events.description: Corporations are weaponizing your personal data to inflate prices, suppress wages, and undermine transparency and basic fairness. This panel will expose how a convergence of corporate power and new technology has created a dizzying array of deceptive pricing practices. From surveillance pricing on airfares to algorithmic price fixing by landlords, companies are leveraging troves of consumer data to rip off customers. Our panelists will share how they have successfully defeated some of these nefarious practices and what policymakers, regulators, and consumers can do to fight back.

name: Lisa Marsch
title: Director, Center for Technology and Behavioral Health

events.name: Unleashing Students' Tech Genius to Ensure Mental Wellness
events.description: Panelists will discuss emerging tech-driven mental health solutions and the importance of involving end users – in this case, college students – in their development. Centered on the student-led creation of a first-of-its-kind AI tool to support mental health, this discussion with the project’s researchers and students will emphasize the need to let research guide how we integrate AI into healthcare to harness its benefits without creating new harms, all while empowering students to become ethical stewards of AI.

events.name: Featured Session: Launch, Land, Orbit: The Future of Space with Firefly
events.description: A fireside chat with Jason Kim, CEO, and Brigette Oakes, VP of Engineering, at Firefly Aerospace. In 2025, Firefly became the first commercial company to successfully land on the Moon, and now the company is working towards a repeat in 2026. Firefly’s next lunar is bigger and bolder, set to land on the far side of the Moon for the first time in American history. This company is simultaneously launching rockets to fill a void in the growing satellite market while also preparing to orbit a constellation of spacecraft in support of in-space maneuverability, space domain awareness, and more. Jason and Brigette will discuss what’s next for the company and where the space industry is headed next.

events.name: Loren Grush Book Signing
events.description: Loren Grush will be signing copies of “The Six” at the SXSW Bookstore.

name: Lynn Pasquerella
title: President
company: American Association Of Colleges And Universities

events.name: Who Gets to Shape the Future of AI? Talent, Policy & Power
events.description: AI is reshaping every industry—but will America remain the world’s innovation engine? As visa rules tighten and geopolitical suspicion grows, top AI talent is being diverted elsewhere. This panel explores how U.S. immigration, research, and tech policy decisions are influencing the global AI race. Drawing on history and current data, we’ll ask: Can the U.S. still attract the world’s best minds—or will they build the future somewhere else?

events.name: Seeing Is Believing: The Vision Behind Humanoid & Robotic AI
events.description: Humanoids and robots are moving from science fiction to reality, and vision is key to that evolution. Just as our visual cortex allows humans to perceive and adapt, machine vision gives robots the ability to understand, navigate, and interact with the world around them. This session explores how advances in depth sensing, AI compute, and cloud-scale learning are giving humanoids and robots sight, safety, and awareness to operate naturally among us—and what that means for the future of human-robot interaction.

events.name: Safety and Security in the Age of AI Autonomy
events.description: We’re entering an era where AI doesn’t just answer questions—it takes action. From calendar scheduling to full-stack coding, agentic systems are gaining autonomy, unlocking powerful capabilities and introducing novel risks. How do we secure systems that can act on our behalf? Who’s accountable when they fail? In this presentation, Dhamani and Engler, co-authors of the bestselling book Introduction to Generative AI, explore the future of safety, security, and human oversight in a world where AI systems don’t just assist us—they act for us.

name: Mahesh Saptharishi
title: Executive Vice President and Chief Technology Officer

events.name: Designing AI for the Worst Day of Your Life
events.description: Would you trust AI to save your life? Join Motorola Solutions and Anthropic to explore designing AI for your worst day. Discover how rigorously boundary testing AI in life-or-death situations—where seconds save lives—reveals critical truths for AI safety. Unpack "good friction" and essential controls to prevent harmful AI, including automation bias. Learn frameworks for responsible human-AI collaboration applicable across any industry, building AI you can stake your life on.

events.name: Smart Talks Live: Brewing Smarter with HEINEKEN and IBM
events.description: How is HEINEKEN transforming itself into the world’s best‑connected brewer? Malcolm Gladwell and HEINEKEN’s Chief AI Officer, Surajeet Ghosh, sit down for a live episode of Smart Talks with IBM podcast to explore how the company is using data, AI and strong partnerships to create better experiences for customers and consumers worldwide. Hear how HEINEKEN is modernizing its digital backbone, empowering its people, and unlocking new ways to innovate in the future of beer.

events.name: Featured Session: The Internet of Value Meets the Internet of Intelligence
events.description:
As AI and blockchain mature in parallel, we are witnessing the emergence of two powerful networks: the Internet of Intelligence, where autonomous AI systems reason and act, and the Internet of Value, where tokenized assets and stablecoins move frictionlessly across borders.

name: Manuel Garcia Garcia
title: Global Lead of Science Activation, Research and Strategy

events.name: Smarter Together: The Cognitive Toolkit for Better AI
events.description: As AI reshapes how we think, feel, and decide, this workshop explores how marketers, researchers, and creators can design more human-centered AI systems. Blending neuroscience, behavioral science, and real-world case studies, we’ll unpack the psychology behind trust, emotion, and collaboration in AI. Participants will leave with a framework for building AI that enhances—not replaces—human insight, helping teams navigate this new era with curiosity, empathy, and practical wisdom.

events.name: Mentor Session: Marc Nathan (Michael Best)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Funding, Food/AgTech, Business Development.

events.name: Superconnector Meet Up
events.description: Superconnectors' - those who seem hyper-connected to large networks of people - are naturally drawn to SXSW. They thrive in a creative and innovative environment like SXSW which affords countless opportunities to meet interesting people. In its third year at SXSW, the Superconnectors Meet Up is intentionally designed to give business development professionals a place to network with other business leaders.

events.name: What Happens When Software Can Transact Autonomously?
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description:
Autonomous AI agents are moving beyond recommendations to making decisions and executing transactions on our behalf—reshaping the shopping journey itself. As commerce becomes software-mediated, experiences are increasingly optimized for machines, not humans, changing discovery, choice, and brand interaction. While agent-led commerce reduces friction, it raises new questions around trust and accountability.

This session explores how agentic commerce reshapes incentives for merchants, platforms, developers, and consumers, and introduces the technical frameworks enabling agent-led transactions.

events.name: 10 Truths About Technology That Are Shaping Your Life
events.description: Every day, invisible systems shape your decisions, desires, attention, and identity, often without your awareness. This session uncovers 10 hidden truths about how algorithms, AI models, and digital platforms subtly reprogram human behavior. Through real-world examples and sharp analysis, this talk explores how technology influences us psychologically and culturally, and what we can do to remain conscious, critical, and truly in control.

events.name: 10 Truths About Technology That Are Shaping Your Life
events.description: Every day, invisible systems shape your decisions, desires, attention, and identity, often without your awareness. This session uncovers 10 hidden truths about how algorithms, AI models, and digital platforms subtly reprogram human behavior. Through real-world examples and sharp analysis, this talk explores how technology influences us psychologically and culturally, and what we can do to remain conscious, critical, and truly in control.

events.name: Can Media Survive AI? The Fight for Public Trust
events.description: AI is reshaping how information is created, distributed, and trusted. But it’s not just a threat - it’s an opportunity to reimagine what media can be. We will explore how AI is transforming business models, content incentives, and consumer expectations. From the rise of creators to the reinvention of trust, we’ll unpack what’s breaking, what’s possible, and how to build what’s next.

events.name: Are you faster than a robot?
events.description: Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe and CrunchLabs Founder Mark Rober host the ultimate autonomous robot challenge. This interactive presentation pits audience members against the robot brainchild RJ and Mark teamed up to build, testing human reactions vs machine learning. Can people compete with computers? How is Rivian approaching autonomy? Are people really that slow?!?!?? We’ll find out.

events.name: Featured Session: The Science of Scaling: Using Data to Decide When—and How Fast—to Scale Revenue
events.description:
Are You Ready to Scale Sales? How Fast?
These two questions are mission-critical to the success of any startup, product launch, or market expansion. Yet, too often we rely on gut feel—or let irrelevant signals like a recent fundraise or comparisons to past unicorns— to drive our decisions.
The Science of Scaling offers a rigorous framework for founders, executives, and investors to calculate the answers using their company’s actual performance data—not wishful thinking.
Drawing on insights from hundreds of startups over the past 25 years, Mark Roberge—Founding CRO at HubSpot, Senior Lecturer at Harvard Business School, and Co-Founder of Stage 2 Capital—reveals the five most common reasons revenue acceleration efforts fail.
Whether you're a founder starting to scale, an investor guiding your portfolio, or a GM launching a new product, The Science of Scaling framework is your operating manual.
Don’t guess. Don’t gamble. Scale scientifically.

events.name: Mark Roberge Book Signing
events.description: Mark Roberge will be signing copies of “The Science of Scaling” at the SXSW Bookstore.

events.name: Waveform: The MKBHD Podcast Live
events.description:
Join Marques Brownlee for a live-taping of his podcast, Waveform: The MKBHD Podcast. Better known as MKBHD, Brownlee has made a name for himself on YouTube reviewing everything from the newest smartphones to electric cars. Pulling from over 10 years of experience covering the tech industry, MKBHD and co-hosts Andrew Manganelli and David Imel will take a deep dive into the latest in tech and what deserves your hard earned cash. 

Part of the Vox Media Podcast Stage at SXSW.

events.name: Featured Session: How to Support Resilient Youth in an AI World
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description:
Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how young people learn, connect, and understand themselves. From AI tutors and creative tools to always-on digital companions, these technologies promise new opportunities, but also raise urgent questions about children’s cognitive, social, and emotional development. Rather than focusing on whether AI should be used in education, how do we ensure AI strengthens—rather than undermines—young people’s capacity to think, relate, and thrive?

Drawing from the recent Brookings report "A New Direction for Students in an AI World: Prosper, Prepare, Protect,” with data across 50 countries, this panel will include a rich mix of perspectives and explore what it truly means to support resilient youth in an AI-saturated world.

events.name: Mentor Session: Martin Rabaglia (Genosha)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Content Strategy/Production, Future of Work, Immersive Technology, Storytelling, Coding, Marketing/Advertising/PR, Entrepreneurship.

events.name: F*ck Bauhaus: A Global Meetup to Recode Creative Work Meet Up
events.description: This is for designers, strategists, technologists, and creative misfits from around the world who refuse to let automation flatten our craft. We’ve designed a dynamic format: a built to spark real connection, fast idea exchange, and collective learning from every person in the room. Whether you want to reshape your creative code, challenge the algorithm, or just find your global crew this is your table.

events.name: XR Immersive Filmmaking & The Future of Entertainment
events.description: XR is not just a new format—it’s a new language of interactive cinematic storytelling. Join director Doug Liman’s award-winning team 30 Ninjas as they explore how working with Google XR teams they are combining emerging formats like volumetric capture, spatial narrative, 3D engines and real-time Google LLM driven characters are reshaping entertainment from the inside out. Learn how cinematic craft, game mechanics, XR and AI are merging at the highest level to create the future of audience experience and participation—where the viewer isn’t just watching the story, they’re inside it.

events.name: Operating Systems of the 3rd Millennium: AI + XR + NEURO
events.description: Neural interfaces are entering a new phase where AI algorithms read brain signals in real-time and power adaptive feedback loops. This panel convenes OpenBCI and cross-disciplinary experts to show how AI turns raw brain signals into actionable insights, powers real-time closed-loop haptics and visuals, and scales from lab demos to consumer and research-grade neurotech. We’ll dissect technical hurdles, safety imperatives, and the roadmap to trustworthy neurotechnology.

events.name: Matt Carmichael Book Signing
events.description: Matt Carmichael will be signing copies of “Meeting My Heroes” at the SXSW Bookstore.

events.name: Foresight Improv
events.description:
Do you wish you could predict the future? Come challenge our panel of futurists as they try to do it themselves! 

In 2024, SXSW attendees put a panel of futurists’ knowledge to the test, challenging them to improvise their view on the future of 18 audience-selected topics from toilet paper to war to thinking. Attendees submitted ideas into a fishbowl that spurred a constructive view of the future of a topic, in 3 minutes or less. This year we hope to return bigger and better with some new tricks and twists. Expect a rousing, dynamic, fun-filled adventure into the future and beyond!

events.name: See Where the Money Goes: Rebuild Trust in Humanitarian Aid
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description:
Trust is under pressure across industries, but in the humanitarian sector it has become existential. As public skepticism around waste, corruption, and opaque systems grows, support for aid can disappear fast—as seen in 2025 with sweeping cuts to U.S. foreign assistance driven by accountability concerns.

This session explores how blockchain is reshaping humanitarian aid by enabling real-time, verifiable tracking of stablecoin-based funds from donor to recipient. We’ll examine what trust-by-design systems mean not just for aid, but for any sector grappling with accountability at scale.

events.name: AI Infrastructure: Building Stargate & Beyond
events.description:
The AI revolution has changed how we work, play, and more. It is also driving a major shift in the backend infrastructure that powers these developments.

As AI capabilities accelerate, leading tech companies are moving beyond software and into the physical world of data centers, power, and infrastructure. Why? Because the future of intelligence now depends as much on electricity and silicon as it does on algorithms.

From energy partnerships to site selection, this conversation will reveal how data center development has shaped the landscape in nearby locations – such as OpenAI’s Stargate facility in Abilene, Texas – and perhaps someday far away in Earth’s orbit.

events.name: Mentor Session: Matthew Celia (Light Sail VR)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Content Strategy/Production, Immersive Technology, XR.

events.name: Intelligence on Tap: Designing the Frontier Firm
events.description: Welcome to the era of intelligence on demand. Microsoft’s Work Trend Index introduces the Frontier Firm: built on abundant AI, where digital agents aren’t tools, they’re teammates. While some fear AI replacing jobs, the real risk is ignoring it. With 80% of employees maxed out and 82% of leaders betting on AI, the question isn’t if you’ll use digital labor, it’s how. Frontier Firms are rethinking org charts, hiring AI trainers, and managing human-agent ratios like headcount. This is your blueprint for building a business where AI scales work and where human judgment stays at the center.

name: Matthew Jensen
title: Global Head of Government Relations and Public Policy

events.name: Meet Your New Work Team: AI, Chatbots, and the Workplace
events.description: "Hello, how can I help you?" Standard questions that once started human conversations are now chatbot prompts. As AI embeds in workplaces worldwide, how are these tools transforming professional interactions and workplace culture? What risks are encoded in systems mediating communication between colleagues and customers? This panel examines the EU's framework for compliant AI deployment, emerging hiring trends, and shifting skill demands. From voice interfaces to decision-making algorithms, discover how workplace AI is being designed-and what it means for workers, employers, and society.

events.name: Featured Session: The Internet After Search
events.description:
The economic model that has funded the Internet for three decades is breaking. AI systems now provide answers directly instead of sending users to websites, and AI agents are beginning to handle transactions without people ever visiting a site. Content creators are losing traffic and revenue, and nobody has figured out what replaces it.

As CEO and co-founder of Cloudflare, Matthew Prince has a unique view of how the web is changing and what comes next, handling 20% of all Internet traffic. This session will examine the post-search Internet and the profound shift in power. We’ll dive into how AI agents are changing commerce, who truly controls information access, and the looming question of who gets paid for content. The choices we make now regarding compensation, ownership, and digital access will determine the web's future trajectory.

events.name: The Writers Room LIVE
events.description: A behind-the-scenes look inside a real comedy writers’ room with Seth Green, his Robot Chicken co-creator, and his writing team. Watch as they break stories, chase punch lines, and riff in real time onstage, using Superhuman as a creative collaborator.

events.name: AI Builders Meet Up
events.description: Join fellow founders, engineers, researchers, and product leaders who are building the next generation of AI products. This casual meetup brings together hands-on innovators to swap stories, share tools, and talk about what’s working (and what’s not) in the fast-moving world of AI. Whether you’re training models, shipping products, or scaling teams—come meet your peers and compare notes

events.name: Vibe Coding: From Idea to MVP at Warp Speed
events.description: Forget decks and buzzwords—this is a build-first session. In this interactive workshop, founders will use Vibe Coding, a fast, intuition-driven method for turning ideas into real products. Using LEGO® to spark rapid collaboration and system-level thinking, participants will physically map app components before we Vibe Code them into working software. Along the way, we’ll explore what it actually takes to build an enterprise-ready app—covering usability, security, reliability, and scalability—so speed doesn’t come at the expense of quality. Expect hands-on collaboration, high energy, and an open-source prototype you can keep building after SXSW. No coding expertise required—just ideas, creativity, and collaboration.

events.name: Star‑Powered Startups: Hits, Misses & Messes
events.description: This panel builds on last year’s exploration of incubation models to spotlight how celebrities today are acting as startup founders, investors, and brand builders. We’ll unpack the good, bad and ugly details of celebrity‑backed ventures—from Skims to Goop—exploring brand fit, media power, product execution, partnership pitfalls, and long‑term viability. Attendees will gain actionable insights into how celebrity involvement amplifies opportunity… and risk.

events.name: U.S. vs. The World: the Future We’re (Currently) Losing
events.description: Is the United States losing its edge in global innovation? In this high-energy continuation of last year’s SXSW session, Congressman Gabe Amo and VC Matthew Weinberg delve further into how Trump-era research cuts, clean energy rollbacks, and talent restrictions have created lasting consequences for U.S. competitiveness and opened the door for China to surge ahead. Blending policy insight with on-the-ground investor perspective, this advanced fireside chat asks: How do we revamp a system with no Plan B, and what bold, bipartisan moves can win our future back before it’s too late?

events.name: Featured Session: How to Support Resilient Youth in an AI World
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description:
Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how young people learn, connect, and understand themselves. From AI tutors and creative tools to always-on digital companions, these technologies promise new opportunities, but also raise urgent questions about children’s cognitive, social, and emotional development. Rather than focusing on whether AI should be used in education, how do we ensure AI strengthens—rather than undermines—young people’s capacity to think, relate, and thrive?

Drawing from the recent Brookings report "A New Direction for Students in an AI World: Prosper, Prepare, Protect,” with data across 50 countries, this panel will include a rich mix of perspectives and explore what it truly means to support resilient youth in an AI-saturated world.

name: Maurice Conti
title: AI Visionary, Futurist and Global Keynote Speaker, Applied Intelligence

events.name: Future-Proof Your Strategy: An AI Test and Learn Lab
events.description: Forget fuzzy predictions. It’s time to get real. What if AI doesn’t unfold the way we expect? In this hands-on, high-energy workshop, you’ll test your strategy against four radically different futures: Growth, Constraint, Transform, and Collapse. You’ll game out scenarios, stress-test decisions, and build a future-ready playbook to lead—not just react—through AI’s uncertainty. Walk away with fresh insights, a sharper strategic lens, and tools to navigate the complexity and opportunity ahead.

events.name: The End of SDV: Building an AI-Defined Vehicle
events.description: We’ve spent a decade making cars "smartphones on wheels," but the next era is about AI-defined mobility. And while the industry talks about "software-defined vehicles," the real breakthrough lies in the marriage of high-performance compute and intelligent systems. This conversation explores how Rivian is building a proprietary hardware-software stack to create vehicles that don't just react to the world, but actively anticipate the needs of the adventurer. We’re moving beyond "smart" features toward a world where the car’s local processing power drives performance and supports safety features and presence, even at the edge of the map.

events.name: It Writes Itself: A Live AI Comedy Variety Hour
events.description: A fast-moving, comedy experiment where performers, the audience, and Superhuman co-create live on stage. With sketches, music, storytelling, games, and surprises, the show writes itself in real time. Anything can happen. Chaos encouraged.Hosted by Mike Jacobson & Sami Kriegstein JacobsonGuest appearances by Nick Rutherford, Eric Andre & Maylee Todd

events.name: Building AI Pathways for HBCU Talent & Community Impact
events.venue: Austin Marriott Downtown - Waller Ballroom D
events.description: As AI rapidly reshapes education and work, HBCUs sit at a critical intersection of talent development, cultural stewardship, and community accountability. This panel brings together higher education, industry, and ecosystem leaders to examine how ethical AI pathways can be built with HBCUs—not around them. Panelists will explore practical models for aligning curriculum, workforce pipelines, industry partnerships, and community impact so AI innovation leads to access, ownership, and long-term opportunity for Black talent and communities.

events.name: Event Tech Meet Up
events.description:
Are you passionate about the intersection of technology and events? Whether you're at the forefront of managing event technologies, actively involved in producing software for event management, or simply intrigued by the innovative world of event tech, this Meet Up is for you.

What to Expect:
-Networking Opportunities
-Industry Insights
-Collaboration Opportunities

Don't miss this opportunity to expand your network, gain valuable insights, and be a part of shaping the future of event technology.

name: Michael Giannangeli
title: Head Of Product, Agentic AI - Amazon Nova

events.name: Making a Mind Live from SXSW: When AI Agents Leave the Lab
events.description: Everyone's talking about AI agents, but what actually happens when companies deploy them to do real work? This live recording of the "Making a Mind" podcast explores the gap between how we think agentic AI should work and how it actually behaves in the wild. Cognitive scientist and podcast host Dr. Danielle Perszyk sits down with Michael Giannangeli, head of product, agentic AI for Amazon Nova, plus industry voices from Amanda Doerr, vice president of core shopping at Amazon and more who've recently launched agents into production. Together, they'll dissect real implementation stories—the successes, the surprises, and the moments where human expectations collided with AI reality. We'll explore the fundamental questions emerging as AI agents become collaborators: Where do humans want control versus autonomy? How do we build trust with systems whose reasoning we can't see? And how does cognitive science research translate into product decisions—from tool-calling architecture to the design choices that determine when agents act independently versus ask for help? This isn't a demo. It's an honest conversation about what we're learning as agentic AI moves from theory to practice—and what it reveals about both artificial and human intelligence.

events.name: Real-world Immersion at Scale: the Future of Spatial
events.description: Dive into the future of real‑world immersion as we explore how XR transforms theaters, museums, retail, and enterprise spaces into living digital playgrounds. This session dives into spatial computing, mixed reality, interactive environments, and AR wearables, revealing how creators reshape physical spaces into dynamic, programmable worlds. Experience how XR blurs physical and digital boundaries and stay for live demos showcasing these innovations. These hands‑on demonstrations will highlight how immersive technologies can transform familiar locations into unforgettable shared adventures.

events.name: Zero-Gravity, Zero Barriers: Future of Inclusive Spaceflight
events.description: Discover what true Space accessibility means at this groundbreaking SXSW panel. Experts—including the first deaf Asian female pilot and aspiring astronaut, the founder of the Blind Institute of Technology, and a Space leader championing disability inclusion—reveal what it will take to make Space open to all. Explore innovations in pilot training, zero-gravity research, AI-assisted interfaces, and adaptive prosthetics. Learn how inclusive Space design inspires broader accessibility on Earth, reshaping our vision of who belongs in the final frontier.

name: Michael Hyzy
title: Vice President Of Ai Strategy And Innovation

events.name: Mike Hyzy Book Signing
events.description: Mike Hyzy will be signing copies of “Gamification for Product Excellence” at the SXSW Bookstore.

events.name: Artistic Disruption for Business Innovation
events.description: Unlock radical imagination with avant-garde creativity methods from Chicago’s Futurist Salon. In this interactive workshop for innovation and culture leaders, you’ll apply speculative avant-garde techniques—surrealist games (metaphor jamming, automatic writing), situationist exercises (dérive-style cultural mapping), and futurist scenario play—to reframe challenges, discover unseen connections, and spark breakthrough ideas. You’ll leave with hands-on tools and a simple framework for hosting your own salons that shift mental models and drive bold innovation across your organization.

events.name: The New Growth Playbook: Activate Consulting’s 2026 Tech & Media Outlook
events.description: The global internet, media, and technology industries are entering a new era—one that will unlock more than $1.3 trillion in incremental revenue by 2029. But as silos disappear and competition intensifies, the rules of growth are being rewritten. In this session, Michael J. Wolf, Founder and CEO of Activate Consulting, presents the firm’s 2026 Tech & Media Outlook, revealing how leading companies must rethink strategy, product, and consumer engagement to stay ahead. Learn which trends will define the next phase of the digital economy—and why winning in 2026 will depend on owning attention across every moment of the consumer’s day.

events.name: Smarter Together: The Cognitive Toolkit for Better AI
events.description: As AI reshapes how we think, feel, and decide, this workshop explores how marketers, researchers, and creators can design more human-centered AI systems. Blending neuroscience, behavioral science, and real-world case studies, we’ll unpack the psychology behind trust, emotion, and collaboration in AI. Participants will leave with a framework for building AI that enhances—not replaces—human insight, helping teams navigate this new era with curiosity, empathy, and practical wisdom.

events.name: Artistic Disruption for Business Innovation
events.description: Unlock radical imagination with avant-garde creativity methods from Chicago’s Futurist Salon. In this interactive workshop for innovation and culture leaders, you’ll apply speculative avant-garde techniques—surrealist games (metaphor jamming, automatic writing), situationist exercises (dérive-style cultural mapping), and futurist scenario play—to reframe challenges, discover unseen connections, and spark breakthrough ideas. You’ll leave with hands-on tools and a simple framework for hosting your own salons that shift mental models and drive bold innovation across your organization.

This live recording of the "Making a Mind" podcast explores the gap between how we think agentic AI should work and how it actually behaves in the wild. Cognitive scientist and podcast host Dr. Danielle Perszyk sits down with Michael Giannangeli, head of product, agentic AI for Amazon Nova, plus industry voices from Amanda Doerr, vice president of core shopping at Amazon and more who've recently launched agents into production.
