And how does cognitive science research translate into product decisions—from tool-calling architecture to the design choices that determine when agents act independently versus ask for help? This isn't a demo. It's an honest conversation about what we're learning as agentic AI moves from theory to practice—and what it reveals about both artificial and human intelligence.

events.name: Featured Session: Actionable Ikigai: Career Planning in the age of AI
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description:
Most career advice stinks.

Why? Because we can’t manage what we don’t measure, and most of us are flying blind.

Join futurist and three-time SXSW fan favorite Mike Bechtel as he turns his foresight toolkit inward and demystifies the future of career planning in the age of AI with a flight-tested dashboard he’s used himself for the past 20 years.

Over the course of this session, Mike will offer the audience tips, techniques, and even take-home-tech to help them determine their "reason-for-being“, plot a practical path to get there, and critically, know when to make course corrections.

Tuned towards early to mid-stage professionals, with relevance to students and senior leaders seeking a recalibration, this fast, story-rich session blends personality with practicality, giving today’s audience the mindset and toolset to design more intentional futures.

events.name: It Writes Itself: AI Variety Show & Creative Workshop
events.description: Based on the “mind-expanding” AI variety show that sold out in NYC, LA, and CDMX, and fresh off of runs at Meta Connect, Disney, and Unilever, IT WRITES ITSELF is “an AI creativity workshop for people who hate workshops.” Part playground, part performance, it’s an interactive mash-up of AI experiments, games, stories and surprises tailored to the folks in the room, resulting in a fun, funny, weird, and occasionally existential workshop like none other. You’ll leave with your brain rewired, curiosity lit up, and a newfound way to explore AI that’s actually fun.

events.name: CTRL + ALT + CREATE: How AI Is Reshaping the Creative Process
events.description: A tech titan and a touring musician dig into how AI is making us reconsider creativity—fueling ideas, speeding production, and widening access, while also raising concerns about ownership, authenticity, and sameness. This candid conversation explores where AI elevates human imagination, where it threatens it, and how creators can keep art innovative and human.

events.name: Thrive or Survive: Why Creativity is the Key to an AI-Future
events.description: AI may make life easier, but at what cost to our imagination? Like a bike we stop riding, our creativity weakens when we overrely on AI. Still, while adults may feel rusty, we can get back on. For children, the risk is even greater: if they never build that muscle in the first place—never learn to ride at all—there’s nothing to return to. This all comes just as jobs are being disrupted, problems grow more complex, and we need more imagination, not less. This panel explores how we can grow creatively with AI (versus be diminished by it) and what we must build now to make that future possible.

events.name: Featured Session: How to Support Resilient Youth in an AI World
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description:
Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how young people learn, connect, and understand themselves. From AI tutors and creative tools to always-on digital companions, these technologies promise new opportunities, but also raise urgent questions about children’s cognitive, social, and emotional development. Rather than focusing on whether AI should be used in education, how do we ensure AI strengthens—rather than undermines—young people’s capacity to think, relate, and thrive?

Drawing from the recent Brookings report "A New Direction for Students in an AI World: Prosper, Prepare, Protect,” with data across 50 countries, this panel will include a rich mix of perspectives and explore what it truly means to support resilient youth in an AI-saturated world.

events.name: Future Tense: A Vibecoding Experience for Educators
events.description: After 3+ years of AI panels and predictions, most people still haven't experienced the technology in a way that makes the stakes visceral. This hands-on workshop changes that. Participants will "vibe code"—building functional software through conversation with AI, no technical background required. You'll create your first learning tool in 10 minutes, iterate with peers, and leave with a working prototype. We'll close by exploring what this means for the future of learning and the future of work: What happens when anyone can build software? What skills matter when routine tasks get automated? Less philosophizing, more co-creating.

events.name: Exotic Thinking: Outsmarting AI with Human Creativity
events.description: In the foresight world, staying brilliant in the Age of the Algorithm demands more than just tech fluency—it requires radical creativity. In this book reading, technofuturist Sarah DaVanzo and AI pioneer Miro Dimitrov explore how we can outthink AI using paradox, taboo, and cognitive disobedience. Drawing from Sarah’s forthcoming book Exotic Thinking, the session offers a provocative path to rewiring human ingenuity in a future shaped by machines.

events.name: AI Safety & Trust: Building Responsible Media Tech
events.description: As AI becomes central to media and entertainment, safety and trust are non-negotiable. This session explores practical steps for building and scaling trustworthy AI, including model testing, transparency, and regulatory compliance.

name: Nabil Bukhari
title: President, AI Platforms and Chief Technology Officer

events.name: Why Most Companies Will Never Be AI Native
events.description: AI-native has become the goal, but for many organizations it’s unrealistic or unnecessary. This conversation will look honestly at what AI-native really means and why teams get stuck trying to achieve it. We’ll explore the technical, organizational, and human bottlenecks that hold companies back, and how strong leaders decide when to invest, when to adapt, and when to walk away. A grounded discussion about tradeoffs, judgment, and practical AI outcomes.

events.name: Mentor Session: Nancy Giordano (Play Big Inc)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Future of Work, Ethics, Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning.

events.name: Mentor Session: Naresh Lalchandani (Shubh Solutions LLC)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Architecture, Storytelling.

name: Natalie Cone
title: Head Of Openai Forum, Global Affairs

events.name: OpenAI & Spurs: Building Human-Centered AI for Fans, Partners, and Communities
events.description: How do you scale AI without losing the human touch? In this session, leaders from the San Antonio Spurs and OpenAI share how they’re using AI to create more meaningful experiences for fans, partners, and communities. From personalized fan engagement and family-focused experiences to internal productivity and decision-making, the Spurs offer a real-world look at how purpose-built AI tools are being used on and off the court. Attendees will walk away with practical lessons on responsible AI adoption, concrete use cases from a global sports franchise, and a blueprint for putting people first while bringing cutting-edge technology to the courtside.

events.name: AI is Changing Consumer Behavior: How Brands Must Adapt
events.description: AI is quietly but profoundly changing how people think, act, and decide. From planning trips to seeking therapy, it’s quickly becoming the first stop for answers and everyday decision-making. It’s also reshaping cognition: one MIT study found that frequent ChatGPT users reported higher loneliness and reduced critical thinking over time. This session brings together behavioral experts, cognitive researchers and marketers to explore how AI is rewiring consumer behavior, and how brands can keep up.

events.name: AI, Your Brain & the Battle for Cognitive Sovereignty
events.description: AI tools rewrite emails, generate ideas, even decide what we see and when. But will they make us lazier thinkers—or force us to think beyond? Will it make us think more clearly or manipulate our perspectives? Will it widen the cognitive gap between rich and poor? Are our neuro-rights protected by design—or will screen time just be optimized again? In this live debate, you’ll pick a side: is AI expanding or eroding our minds? Expect sharp provocations, real-time participation, and one rule: you can’t outsource your thinking here. Join the battle for cognitive sovereignty.

events.name: Thrive or Survive: Why Creativity is the Key to an AI-Future
events.description: AI may make life easier, but at what cost to our imagination? Like a bike we stop riding, our creativity weakens when we overrely on AI. Still, while adults may feel rusty, we can get back on. For children, the risk is even greater: if they never build that muscle in the first place—never learn to ride at all—there’s nothing to return to. This all comes just as jobs are being disrupted, problems grow more complex, and we need more imagination, not less. This panel explores how we can grow creatively with AI (versus be diminished by it) and what we must build now to make that future possible.

events.name: Early Days Again: What AI Makes Possible for New Schools
events.description:
AI tools are improving quickly, and educators are already finding meaningful uses for them. What hasn’t been defined yet is the school model that makes AI work for students and teachers in a coherent, reliable way at scale.

This session examines what early adopters of AI in public education are learning in real classrooms and school systems, the concrete design choices and conditions behind early success, and the operational, instructional, and governance questions that must be resolved over the next five years for AI to strengthen public education rather than fragment it.

events.name: Orchestrate or Obsolete: Leading the AI-Powered Near Future
events.description:
The problem isn’t AI — it’s our leadership model. We’re still leading like humans are the only ones in the room… as the room quietly fills with AI agents.

In the very near future, every decision will be co-authored by humans, AI agents, and live ecosystem signals — and the leaders who master this orchestration will rewrite how business works.

Near Futurist Neil Redding shows how leadership must evolve in the Agentic Era, from command and control to the orchestration of human–AI symbiosis. You’ll leave with a clear mental model for leading in systems where humans and AI participate together, plus daily leadership rituals for thriving as business capabilities evolve in real time.

events.name: Brains & Bots: Evolving the Future of Intelligence Together
events.description: Does offloading thinking to AI cause cognitive atrophy or free our brains to think about more complex problems? That’s the million-neuron question. This provocative panel explores “adaptive intelligence”, where human and machine learning loops converge. A neuroscientist, futurist, and AI visionary unpack how co-learning systems, neuromorphic design, and spatial computing can amplify creativity, decision-making, and human agency in a world where algorithms don’t just support us, they shape us.

events.name: Featured Session: 10 Breakthrough Technologies of 2026
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description: Each year, MIT Technology Review reporters and editors assemble a list of the top breakthrough technologies that will change the world. We ask ourselves: What will really matter in the long run? And while we can’t see the future, we expect these technologies to affect our world in a big way, for decades to come. Niall Firth will share the publication’s list of ten breakthrough technologies with the SXSW audience, explaining each one and how it will impact the way we live and work.

events.name: Revolutionizing Astronomy with Next Generation Big Data
events.description: Massive datasets, like those in astronomy, are the lifeblood of AI innovation.  The panel, led by researchers from the NSF-Simons AI Institute for Cosmic Origins, will discuss how AI is transforming astronomy to unlock new insights into the origins of our universe. They will present visualizations showing state-of-the-art astronomical datasets built on flagship observations and supercomputing simulations of the Universe. They will discuss how these data can be used to develop novel AI methods that accelerate discovery, expanding the frontiers of AI possibility inside and outside astronomy.

events.name: AI in Entertainment: Navigating IP, Ethics & Opportunity
events.description: Explore how generative and agentic AI are transforming entertainment and media, from content creation to distribution.  This session will unpack the legal, ethical, and business challenges—especially around IP, fair use, and talent rights—while highlighting new opportunities for innovation and collaboration.

name: Nick Rutherford
title: Emmy Award-Winning Writer, Comedian, And Producer

events.name: It Writes Itself: A Live AI Comedy Variety Hour
events.description: A fast-moving, comedy experiment where performers, the audience, and Superhuman co-create live on stage. With sketches, music, storytelling, games, and surprises, the show writes itself in real time. Anything can happen. Chaos encouraged.Hosted by Mike Jacobson & Sami Kriegstein JacobsonGuest appearances by Nick Rutherford, Eric Andre & Maylee Todd

events.name: Operating Systems of the 3rd Millennium: AI + XR + NEURO
events.description: Neural interfaces are entering a new phase where AI algorithms read brain signals in real-time and power adaptive feedback loops. This panel convenes OpenBCI and cross-disciplinary experts to show how AI turns raw brain signals into actionable insights, powers real-time closed-loop haptics and visuals, and scales from lab demos to consumer and research-grade neurotech. We’ll dissect technical hurdles, safety imperatives, and the roadmap to trustworthy neurotechnology.

events.name: Mentor Session: Nolan Melson (Apple)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Product Management/Development, Entrepreneurship.

events.name: Mentor Session: Numa Dhamani (iVerify)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Data Science.

events.name: Safety and Security in the Age of AI Autonomy
events.description: We’re entering an era where AI doesn’t just answer questions—it takes action. From calendar scheduling to full-stack coding, agentic systems are gaining autonomy, unlocking powerful capabilities and introducing novel risks. How do we secure systems that can act on our behalf? Who’s accountable when they fail? In this presentation, Dhamani and Engler, co-authors of the bestselling book Introduction to Generative AI, explore the future of safety, security, and human oversight in a world where AI systems don’t just assist us—they act for us.

events.name: AI & the Brain: As We Embrace AI, Let’s Not Forget Our Minds
events.venue: The Westin Austin Downtown - Paramount I-II
events.description:
Across homes, offices, and classrooms, AI is becoming ubiquitous—mapping our routes, writing our code, even dreaming up art in seconds. What once sounded like science fiction is now silently transforming our daily existence. And yet, amid this great digital leap forward, a quieter, more fragile system is at stake: the human brain.
We are perched at a precipice in history. As AI systems grow in power and presence, we must ask a question too few dare to voice: What is this doing to us - our capacity to explore, to learn, to reason, and to create? The machines are learning. But are we forgetting?

events.name: The Future Design Language of Robots
events.description: As robots enter homes, hospitals, and public spaces, their design language — how they look, move, and behave — will shape how we trust and relate to them. This session explores emerging visual, interactional, and behavioral grammars of human-robot interaction. Must robots mimic humans or machines, or can new forms evolve? We’ll examine how design can expand the roles robots play and the relationships we build with them across cultures and contexts.

events.name: 25 Minutes of AI: The SXSW Speed-Briefing
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description:
The AI news cycle doesn’t just move fast—it moves at the speed of culture. In this high-intensity, 25-minute live briefing, Monks takes their signature monthly innovation series from the screen to the stage. This isn't a lecture; it’s a no-nonsense sprint through the breakthroughs, the hype, and the real-world applications actually worth your time.

Designed for the SXSW audience, this session is structured into four rapid-fire acts:
Monthly Wonders: A pulse check on the week’s biggest AI news and hidden gems surfacing in Austin.
The 101 Deep Dive: We peel back the curtain on a complex concept—moving beyond the buzzwords to explain the tech shifting the industry.
Monks in Action: A raw, behind-the-scenes walkthrough of an AI-powered case study, demonstrating how we turn agentic workflows into creative impact.
The Homework: You leave with a tangible task—a specific tool to test or a framework to apply—ensuring you walk out smarter than you walked in.

events.name: GenMedia 101: Unlocking Creativity with AI & Google DeepMind
events.description: A hands-on session for artists, storytellers, and makers of all kinds. Build visuals, music, and media experiences - no code required. Every creative idea starts with a feeling: a scene in your mind, a sound in your head, or a story you keep coming back to. This workshop is for anyone who wants to bring those ideas to life, with a little help from generative AI. You’ll explore how tools like Veo (for video), Lyria (for music), and Nano Banana (for images) can help you shape what you imagine into something you can actually see, hear, and share. Laptop & headphones/earbuds recommended.

events.name: Mentor Session: Pallavi Lobo (Thalus, Inc.)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in "Diversity, Equity & Inclusion", Entrepreneurship, Product Management/Development.

events.name: Who Predicts Better and Why? Humans, AI, and the Anatomy of a Forecast
events.description: AI models process vast signals; humans bring judgment and context. But who calls real-world outcomes better, and when? This session compares human and model forecasts on headline events, drawing on newsroom practice, product experience, and reasoning-centric AI research. We’ll map where models’ pattern breadth wins, where human context does, and why hybrids often work best. Instead of scoreboards alone, we’ll discuss how journalists and analysts should use uncertainty ranges, disclose methods and sources, and avoid feedback loops that can steer the story itself.

events.name: Mentor Session: Parker Wishik (The Aerospace Corporation)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Storytelling, Space Exploration, Marketing/Advertising/PR, Content Strategy/Production.

events.name: The Companies Building and Automating Space's Superhighway
events.description: How do we keep space sustainable as it gets more crowded? Enter the Space Superhighway: a network of in-space robots that will keep spacecraft fueled, repaired, upgraded and built in orbit. Just like highways rely on gas stations, tow trucks and repair shops, space will need support systems to be accessible for future generations. This tech is real TODAY. Our panel convenes accomplished doers from startups working to extend the life of satellites, reduce space debris and build a future where space is safe, scalable and sustainable for exploration and economic development to benefit society.

events.name: Mentor Session: Paul "IX" Kemppainen (SISU)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Entrepreneurship, Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning.

events.name: Who Gets to Shape the Future of AI? Talent, Policy & Power
events.description: AI is reshaping every industry—but will America remain the world’s innovation engine? As visa rules tighten and geopolitical suspicion grows, top AI talent is being diverted elsewhere. This panel explores how U.S. immigration, research, and tech policy decisions are influencing the global AI race. Drawing on history and current data, we’ll ask: Can the U.S. still attract the world’s best minds—or will they build the future somewhere else?

events.name: Revolutionizing Astronomy with Next Generation Big Data
events.description: Massive datasets, like those in astronomy, are the lifeblood of AI innovation.  The panel, led by researchers from the NSF-Simons AI Institute for Cosmic Origins, will discuss how AI is transforming astronomy to unlock new insights into the origins of our universe. They will present visualizations showing state-of-the-art astronomical datasets built on flagship observations and supercomputing simulations of the Universe. They will discuss how these data can be used to develop novel AI methods that accelerate discovery, expanding the frontiers of AI possibility inside and outside astronomy.

events.name: Featured Session: How Crypto is Building New Capital Markets for Everyone, not just Wall Street
events.description: For years, crypto was criticized as a solution searching for a problem. The on-chain creator economy failed to gain momentum, and the concept of ownership reflected less on community governance and more on self-custody. But something shifted. As crypto activity became increasingly financialized, the infrastructure built to support it quietly unlocked something bigger—new pathways for creators and everyday users to participate in capital markets once reserved for institutions. Join Pedro Miranda, Head of Consumer at the Solana Foundation, and Rodolfo Gonzalez from Foundation Capital as we examine what's changed in the on-chain economy and explore the emerging consumer crypto landscape that's democratizing access to financial opportunity.

events.name: AI Infrastructure: Building Stargate & Beyond
events.description:
The AI revolution has changed how we work, play, and more. It is also driving a major shift in the backend infrastructure that powers these developments.

As AI capabilities accelerate, leading tech companies are moving beyond software and into the physical world of data centers, power, and infrastructure. Why? Because the future of intelligence now depends as much on electricity and silicon as it does on algorithms.

In this session, OpenAI pulls back the curtain on what it really takes to scale AI today. The conversation explores how data center development has become mission-critical, how decisions about where to build are made, and what happens when AI growth collides with real-world limits such as energy, land, and supply chains.

From energy partnerships to site selection, this conversation will reveal how data center development has shaped the landscape in nearby locations – such as OpenAI’s Stargate facility in Abilene, Texas – and perhaps someday far away in Earth’s orbit.

name: Peter Stone
title: Chief Scientist and Professor of Computer Science
company: Sony AI and The University of Texas at Austin

events.name: Humanoid Robots Are Clocking In: Meet Who’s Putting Them To Work
events.description: You’ve seen the viral humanoid demos—now meet the people actually shipping them. From hospitals to warehouses, humanoid robots are already on shift. They ride elevators, open doors, and work alongside people in busy environments. But making that leap from prototype to real-world, reliable performance? It’s hard. This panel brings together renowned founders and researchers who’ve built and deployed some of the first real humanoids at scale. Join us for a behind-the-scenes conversation about what's working, what’s not, and what it really takes to get robots out of the lab and into the world.

events.name: Is Reinforcement Learning the Real Future of AI?
events.description: While AGI, generative AI, and humanoid robots dominate the spotlight, reinforcement learning (RL) is quietly powering the next chapter of AI. Unlike other methods, RL enables systems to learn by doing – adapting through trial and error in dynamic environments. In this session, one of the world’s leading RL minds will explore why this approach is essential for building truly autonomous, intelligent systems, and how today’s breakthroughs are laying the groundwork for the next wave of transformative AI.

events.name: Featured Session: David Pogue on Apple’s First 50 Years
events.description: As Apple enters its second half-century, author David Pogue (“Apple: The First 50 Years”) explores the throughlines of Apple’s astonishing success: simplicity, beauty, elegant design, making the whole widget, focus on very few products, and a deep commitment to the customer experience. He’ll be joined by Phil Schiller to share stories and insights from their decades of launching iconic products.

events.name: Money, Sex & Religion - How Design Turns Taboo to Touchpoint
events.description: Sex, money, and religion are no longer taboo—they’re trending. This panel explores how behavioral UX, gamification, and human-centered design can destigmatize what used to be off-limits. From intimacy apps to investing platforms, discover how intentional design is helping users navigate vulnerable moments with confidence. Hear from sex educator Shan Boodram, Alinea’s Gen Z fintech founder Anam Lakhani, Bloomberg’s Riley Griffin, and Play Ventures’ Phylicia Koh—one of few female investors in gaming VC—on how intentional UX is turning discomfort into discovery.

events.name: AI & Culture: Empowering the African Diaspora
events.description: AI is transforming industries, yet cultural representation often lags behind. This session explores how AI can be used to preserve cultural heritage, amplify diverse voices, and create new business models across the African diaspora. Through real-world case studies in entertainment, fashion, and events, we’ll uncover strategies for building inclusive AI ecosystems that merge cultural identity, creativity, and innovation for global impact.

events.name: OpenAI & Spurs: Building Human-Centered AI for Fans, Partners, and Communities
events.description: How do you scale AI without losing the human touch? In this session, leaders from the San Antonio Spurs and OpenAI share how they’re using AI to create more meaningful experiences for fans, partners, and communities. From personalized fan engagement and family-focused experiences to internal productivity and decision-making, the Spurs offer a real-world look at how purpose-built AI tools are being used on and off the court. Attendees will walk away with practical lessons on responsible AI adoption, concrete use cases from a global sports franchise, and a blueprint for putting people first while bringing cutting-edge technology to the courtside.

events.name: Are you faster than a robot?
events.description: Rivian CEO RJ Scaringe and CrunchLabs Founder Mark Rober host the ultimate autonomous robot challenge. This interactive presentation pits audience members against the robot brainchild RJ and Mark teamed up to build, testing human reactions vs machine learning. Can people compete with computers? How is Rivian approaching autonomy? Are people really that slow?!?!?? We’ll find out.

events.name: Electrification: Moving the Needle at Both Ends of the Scale
events.description: Electric vehicles are often discussed as a climate solution or a technological milestone, but their most powerful benefits may be human. Beyond emissions, EVs have the potential to change how our cities sound and feel, how people experience daily movement, and how transportation impacts health, stress, and overall quality of life. In this panel, ALSO President and Co-Founder Chris Yu and Rivian Founder and CEO RJ Scaringe explore the science behind why EV adoption can meaningfully improve everyday life, why EV adoption leads to better public health and more time spent outdoors, and the broader systems required to make electric mobility feel intuitive, enjoyable, and worth choosing every day.

events.name: Harnessing AI to Serve Creators and Customers
events.description: At Amazon MGM Studios and Prime Video, AI is giving filmmakers, writers, and designers tools to move faster, think bigger, and take creative risks. We'll pull back the curtain on how AI is pushing the boundaries of what's achievable — and the story doesn't stop at production. Prime Video is harnessing AI to bring content to more people: localizing stories across languages and cultures, and enhancing the viewing experience so audiences connect more deeply with the content they love. This session will focus on what it means to build AI responsibly, with human creativity as the north star.

events.name: The Future of Work Is Still Human
events.description: As AI reshapes how we work, fundamental questions about voice and individual expression are emerging. Rahul Vohra, Founder of Superhuman Mail, explores why the most powerful tools don’t replace human judgment or flatten individual expression, but expand what people are capable of doing. Grounded in his experience building Superhuman Mail, Rahul discusses how founders and builders can design technology that amplifies human capability while ensuring the work still sounds, feels, and belongs to the user.

events.name: Featured Session: How to Support Resilient Youth in an AI World
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description:
Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how young people learn, connect, and understand themselves. From AI tutors and creative tools to always-on digital companions, these technologies promise new opportunities, but also raise urgent questions about children’s cognitive, social, and emotional development. Rather than focusing on whether AI should be used in education, how do we ensure AI strengthens—rather than undermines—young people’s capacity to think, relate, and thrive?

Drawing from the recent Brookings report "A New Direction for Students in an AI World: Prosper, Prepare, Protect,” with data across 50 countries, this panel will include a rich mix of perspectives and explore what it truly means to support resilient youth in an AI-saturated world.

events.name: Vibe Coding: From Idea to MVP at Warp Speed
events.description: Forget decks and buzzwords—this is a build-first session. In this interactive workshop, founders will use Vibe Coding, a fast, intuition-driven method for turning ideas into real products. Using LEGO® to spark rapid collaboration and system-level thinking, participants will physically map app components before we Vibe Code them into working software. Along the way, we’ll explore what it actually takes to build an enterprise-ready app—covering usability, security, reliability, and scalability—so speed doesn’t come at the expense of quality. Expect hands-on collaboration, high energy, and an open-source prototype you can keep building after SXSW. No coding expertise required—just ideas, creativity, and collaboration.

events.name: 4D Volumetric Video & Gaussian Splats for XR Creators
events.description: Step into the future of content creation with this hands-on workshop exploring 4D Gaussian Splatting—the next evolution in volumetric video. Learn how real-time volumetric capture is transforming live streaming, XR experiences, and immersive storytelling. From filming with multi-camera rigs to compressing, rendering, and deploying in platforms like Unreal Engine, Unity, SuperSplat, and UVRSE, you'll discover how this cutting-edge tech brings people, performances, and places into fully spatialized digital worlds.

events.name: Human Futures: Learning in a Tech-Shaped World
events.description: Through open dialogue, participants will examine how emerging tools reshape learning environments and expand access to knowledge. The meetup invites attendees to interrogate the ways tech products and services are shaping education, challenge assumptions, and imagine new models of collaboration. Through our reporting, we cover how technology sparks more inclusive, creative, culturally relevant experiences outside the West.

events.name: The Clock Is Ticking for Space – Replacing the ISS
events.description: After 30 years of continuous operation, the International Space Station is going away, and fast. What comes next? Enter Axiom Space. Against all odds, this Houston startup flew four crewed commercial missions to the ISS, led record-breaking research, engaged more than 31 nations in the pursuit of space access and partnered with Prada, Oakley, Nokia, and NASA all in pursuit of building the world’s first commercial space station. The dream of living and working in space is no longer distant. Welcome to the space industrialization era—the time is now to transcend Earth.

events.name: Featured Session: How Crypto is Building New Capital Markets for Everyone, not just Wall Street
events.description: For years, crypto was criticized as a solution searching for a problem. The on-chain creator economy failed to gain momentum, and the concept of ownership reflected less on community governance and more on self-custody. But something shifted. As crypto activity became increasingly financialized, the infrastructure built to support it quietly unlocked something bigger—new pathways for creators and everyday users to participate in capital markets once reserved for institutions. Join Pedro Miranda, Head of Consumer at the Solana Foundation, and Rodolfo Gonzalez from Foundation Capital as we examine what's changed in the on-chain economy and explore the emerging consumer crypto landscape that's democratizing access to financial opportunity.

events.name: Featured Session: 5 Non-Obvious Secrets of Human Connection (For Love & Profit)
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description: What will it take to win the future? We’re suffocated by noise, minimized by algorithms, and living in fear of being replaced by technology. The cocoon of technology is making us lose touch with the messy, irreplaceable spark of real connection. In this provocative featured session, SXSW favorite Rohit Bhargava reveals five counterintuitive secrets from his most recent trend research about how we bond, trust, and remain indispensable in the modern world. The people who understand people always win. If you believe this, then this session is your key to becoming one of them.

events.name: The Audacity of Joyosity™: The Surprising ROI of Joy
events.description:
Depletion and disengagement are everywhere. Leaders are in crisis—decision-fatigued, over-optimized, and white-knuckling their way through weeks. AI, efficiency, and metrics aren’t answers.

Joy is.

In this interactive session, leadership and culture expert Jenn Whitmer—with 10x SXSW featured speaker Rohit Bhargava, founder of the Non-Obvious Company—shares stories and research showing how joy drives performance and profit. Get clear on what joy is, why it works at work, and how to use the Joy Ratio to create repeatable results—without grinding harder, toxic positivity, or lowering the bar.

events.name: Rohit Bhargava Book Signing
events.description: Rohit will be signing copies of “Non-Obvious Thinking & Future Normal” at the SXSW Bookstore.

events.name: AI: Friend or Foe of DEIB?
events.description:
Headlines warn that AI is hurting DEIB—propagating bias, reinforcing stereotypes, and risking the erasure of marginalized groups and their histories. Yet the same technology has the potential to uncover bias, scale representation, and raise the bar for inclusion at the brand, industry, academic, and societal levels.

With a consumer-focused lens, panelists from MAC Cosmetics, the UN, NYU, and Responsible AI company, SeeMe Index, will explore both sides of the equation: where AI’s blind spots have failed us, where it’s driving progress, and what it takes to ensure technology reflects the world we want to see.

events.name: AI in Entertainment: Navigating IP, Ethics & Opportunity
events.description: Explore how generative and agentic AI are transforming entertainment and media, from content creation to distribution.  This session will unpack the legal, ethical, and business challenges—especially around IP, fair use, and talent rights—while highlighting new opportunities for innovation and collaboration.

events.name: The End of Work as We Know It: AI, Humans & the New Global Power Dynamics
events.description:
Work is not disappearing.
It is being redistributed — between humans, machines, and the systems that connect them.
As humans become augmented by data and algorithms, the architecture of work — jobs, skills, organizations, and even nations — is being rewritten.
This session explores how AI infrastructure, talent ecosystems, and strategic policy are redefining global power dynamics. 
If work as we knew it is over,
who controls what comes next?  Do we need controls?
And how should leaders, workers, and societies prepare for this shift?

events.name: Zero-Gravity, Zero Barriers: Future of Inclusive Spaceflight
events.description: Discover what true Space accessibility means at this groundbreaking SXSW panel. Experts—including the first deaf Asian female pilot and aspiring astronaut, the founder of the Blind Institute of Technology, and a Space leader championing disability inclusion—reveal what it will take to make Space open to all. Explore innovations in pilot training, zero-gravity research, AI-assisted interfaces, and adaptive prosthetics. Learn how inclusive Space design inspires broader accessibility on Earth, reshaping our vision of who belongs in the final frontier.

name: Ruth Kikin-Gil
title: Responsible AI strategist, Principal UX Designer at Microsoft, Copilot for Security

events.name: The RAI of Every Day Things - from Principles to UX Practice
events.description: Responsible AI isn’t a buzzword—it’s a daily practice that drives the appropriate level of trust, mitigates risk, and powers better business outcomes. But what does it look like in product and design? It’s a practical, human-centered approach to designing AI systems that are safe, equitable, and effective. In this session, RAI leaders from Microsoft, SAP, Adobe, and JPMorgan Chase share real tools, UX strategies, and lessons from the trenches. Gain an understanding of RAI - and walk away with actionable best practices to apply now, whether you're leading solo or scaling across an enterprise.

events.name: Building Reality: Generative AI for the Physical World
events.description: Generative AI has rapidly advanced in text, imagery, and 3D content, but its potential to shape the physical world remains largely untapped. This panel explores how AI can be used to generate designs for things that can be built, manufactured, or constructed, spanning products, machines, buildings, and infrastructure. Experts in computational design, AI research, and digital fabrication will discuss current challenges, technical hurdles, and the foundational work needed to unlock future impact and bring generative design into the realm of the physical.

events.name: Featured Session: The Great Flip: Why Every Industry Is Running Backwards
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description: Technology isn’t just accelerating progress, it's changing the very process of progress itself: product testing can happen virtually before building, clinical trials can be simulated before major lab investment, distribution can dictate where production happens. This session will change how you see every workflow in your business. You'll walk away armed with a practical framework that will help you learn how to identify inversions in your own industry. The future belongs to leaders who think in reverse.

events.name: What AI Agents Are Doing While You're Not Watching
events.description: Your AI assistant has the keys to everything – email, finances, sensitive systems – but operates in shadows you can't see, a problem that continues to grow as companies unleash agentic AI faster than they can build safeguards. This panel will explore how AI agents are changing the way we think about work and how we trust technology. We'll ask: What happens when an AI assistant, designed to help, accidentally (or intentionally) steps out of line? How do we even know what they're up to when they operate independently? And how can we stop AI agents from becoming the next insider threat?

events.name: Why SMB Cyber Attacks Could Cripple the U.S. Economy
events.description: Small and mid-sized businesses (SMBs) account for 99.9% of U.S. businesses and nearly half of GDP, yet their cybersecurity capabilities lag dangerously behind. Nation-state actors and cybercriminals now target SMBs and the managed service providers (MSPs) that support them because one breach can cascade across thousands of businesses, posing a serious risk to national security and economic stability. As AI supercharges social engineering and ransomware at scale, this overlooked attack surface has become one of the most urgent vulnerabilities in America.

name: Sam Seidel
title: Director of Products + Publications; Director of K12 Strategy

events.name: The Knowledge Paradox: When Does Sharing Become Theft?
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description: For decades, the open movement championed sharing as the path to knowledge equity—billions of works freely accessible and reusable across research, education, and culture. But AI weaponizes that openness: what was built to democratize knowledge now trains models that concentrate power and profit. Open data scraping. Closed model development. Free content fueling extraction. As we mark Creative Commons' 25th anniversary, we confront an impossible question: how do we keep knowledge open without enabling its exploitation?

events.name: It Writes Itself: A Live AI Comedy Variety Hour
events.description: A fast-moving, comedy experiment where performers, the audience, and Superhuman co-create live on stage. With sketches, music, storytelling, games, and surprises, the show writes itself in real time. Anything can happen. Chaos encouraged.Hosted by Mike Jacobson & Sami Kriegstein JacobsonGuest appearances by Nick Rutherford, Eric Andre & Maylee Todd

events.name: It Writes Itself: AI Variety Show & Creative Workshop
events.description: Based on the “mind-expanding” AI variety show that sold out in NYC, LA, and CDMX, and fresh off of runs at Meta Connect, Disney, and Unilever, IT WRITES ITSELF is “an AI creativity workshop for people who hate workshops.” Part playground, part performance, it’s an interactive mash-up of AI experiments, games, stories and surprises tailored to the folks in the room, resulting in a fun, funny, weird, and occasionally existential workshop like none other. You’ll leave with your brain rewired, curiosity lit up, and a newfound way to explore AI that’s actually fun.

events.name: See Where the Money Goes: Rebuild Trust in Humanitarian Aid
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description:
Trust is under pressure across industries, but in the humanitarian sector it has become existential. As public skepticism around waste, corruption, and opaque systems grows, support for aid can disappear fast—as seen in 2025 with sweeping cuts to U.S. foreign assistance driven by accountability concerns.

This session explores how blockchain is reshaping humanitarian aid by enabling real-time, verifiable tracking of stablecoin-based funds from donor to recipient. We’ll examine what trust-by-design systems mean not just for aid, but for any sector grappling with accountability at scale.

name: Sandy Carter
title: Chief Business Officer, Founder, and Best Selling Author, Forbes Contributor

events.name: Featured Session: From Pilot to Payoff: 7 Pattern-Matched Traits of AI Systems That Actually Work
events.venue: Hilton Austin Downtown - Hilton Grand Ballroom (Salon HJK)
events.description: Most companies are stuck in “pilot purgatory” with AI. What separates the experiments that stall from the projects that scale? Drawing on research across 450+ companies, Sandy Carter reveals the five common traits that turn AI pilots into ROI-driven successes. Walk away with a roadmap you can apply immediately—whether you’re a startup founder or a Fortune 100 executive.

events.name: AI & the Brain: As We Embrace AI, Let’s Not Forget Our Minds
events.venue: The Westin Austin Downtown - Paramount I-II
events.description:
Across homes, offices, and classrooms, AI is becoming ubiquitous—mapping our routes, writing our code, even dreaming up art in seconds. What once sounded like science fiction is now silently transforming our daily existence. And yet, amid this great digital leap forward, a quieter, more fragile system is at stake: the human brain.
We are perched at a precipice in history. As AI systems grow in power and presence, we must ask a question too few dare to voice: What is this doing to us - our capacity to explore, to learn, to reason, and to create? The machines are learning. But are we forgetting?

events.name: Powerful AI, Terrible UX:  Designing a Smarter UX
events.description: Enterprise AI is getting more powerful, but for many users it still feels slow, awkward, and hard to trust. The problem is rarely the model or the data. It is the experience. Too many tools rely on chat interfaces that add cognitive load, cause prompt fatigue, and fail in high pressure workflows. This session explores why enterprise AI UX keeps breaking down and how cognitive design can reduce friction, rebuild trust, and make AI fit how people actually work.

events.name: Two Futurists and an AI: Seeing Trouble Ahead
events.description: Join celebrity futurist Faith Popcorn, techno-futurist Sarah DaVanzo, and Delph.Ai, an AI Synthetic Human trained on the minds of female futurists in a boundary-pushing, three-way debate on the future of leadership in the age of intelligent machines. With Faith’s dystopian view, Sarah’s utopian counterpoint, and Delph.Ai’s unexpected input, this session explores the trouble coming as AI challenges the very definition of human agency, influence, and power.

events.name: Exotic Thinking: Outsmarting AI with Human Creativity
events.description: In the foresight world, staying brilliant in the Age of the Algorithm demands more than just tech fluency—it requires radical creativity. In this book reading, technofuturist Sarah DaVanzo and AI pioneer Miro Dimitrov explore how we can outthink AI using paradox, taboo, and cognitive disobedience. Drawing from Sarah’s forthcoming book Exotic Thinking, the session offers a provocative path to rewiring human ingenuity in a future shaped by machines.

events.name: Mentor Session: Sarah Endline (DearSarah)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Entrepreneurship, Sustainability.

events.name: The Systems We Build: Business Models for Meaningful XR
events.description: XR is booming… Or is it? How secure are business models in the interactive/immersive sector and how well do they support creators to have social impact? And how do you thrive - not just survive - as a creator, producer, developer of this type of work, right now? Acknowledging challenges and uncertainty but also highlighting opportunities, the panel reflect on their own journeys - including successes, challenges and startings-over - to explore alternative models and offer practical advice for creators and producers to make work that matters: including protecting your IP and your wellbeing.

events.name: Technologies of Care: Ritual, Play, & the Future of Wellness
events.description: This interactive talk will explore how artist-led, science-backed immersive experiences can foster emotional expression and wellbeing. Award-winning artists behind Explore Deep and Soul Paint, Sarah Ticho (Hatsumi, XR Health Alliance) and Niki Smit (Monobanda) share tools for co-creating rituals of care through creative health design. Participants will explore emotional mapping, embodiment, and cross-sector collaboration to imagine new pathways for impact across health, art, and education.

events.name: XR Experience Special Event
XR Experience Special Event
XR Experience Special Event
Co-Creating the Future of XR Distribution
events.description: As immersive media rapidly evolves, we face a critical window of opportunity to shape an equitable distribution ecosystem. Join the Independent XR Distribution Coalition for a participatory workshop on imagining a flourishing XR field. The session will begin with a presentation of distribution pipeline research and a database of venues and platforms, followed by a mapping exercise that surfaces the issues artists, audiences, and producers face in XR. Participants will sketch speculative distribution pathways across diverse platforms, using artist-centered strategies as building blocks.

events.name: Mentor Session: Satish Kumar (ExecLabs)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Product Management/Development, Future of Work.

events.name: Space Technology and Innovation Conversation
events.description: Have an idea that could launch the space industry into its next era? Join a vibrant gathering of space tech pioneers, passionate enthusiasts, and established space professionals. This is your platform to pitch innovative concepts, receive feedback on new and expanding ideas, and forge powerful connections. Come to share, learn, and collaborate with the brightest minds charting the future of space.

events.name: The Future Design Language of Robots
events.description: As robots enter homes, hospitals, and public spaces, their design language — how they look, move, and behave — will shape how we trust and relate to them. This session explores emerging visual, interactional, and behavioral grammars of human-robot interaction. Must robots mimic humans or machines, or can new forms evolve? We’ll examine how design can expand the roles robots play and the relationships we build with them across cultures and contexts.

events.name: Prof G Markets Live
events.description: Prof G Markets breaks down the news that’s moving the capital markets, helping you build financial literacy and security. Join Scott Galloway and Ed Elson for no mercy, no malice insight on high flying stocks, booming sectors, and master of the universe CEOs.

events.name: Pivot Live
events.description: Join tech journalist Kara Swisher and NYU Professor Scott Galloway as they offer sharp, unfiltered insights into the biggest stories in tech, business, and politics. They’ll make bold predictions, pick winners and losers, and bicker and banter like no one else. After all, with great power comes great scrutiny.

events.name: Mentor Session: Sean Evans (EVOQ)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Immersive Technology, XR, Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning.

events.name: The Writers Room LIVE
events.description: A behind-the-scenes look inside a real comedy writers’ room with Seth Green, his Robot Chicken co-creator, and his writing team. Watch as they break stories, chase punch lines, and riff in real time onstage, using Superhuman as a creative collaborator.

events.name: Cultural Conquest: The Digital Black American Blueprint
events.venue: Austin Marriott Downtown - Waller Ballroom B
events.description: In the age of algorithms and automation, what does it mean to protect the essence of Black creativity? This session traces how the history of cultural appropriation is now mirrored through digital platforms, machine learning, and AI-generated content. Grammy-winning producer and strategist Shalom J. Storm Miller presents a future-facing blueprint for cultural integrity, offering educators, creatives, and technologists a framework for sustaining Black influence in an increasingly synthetic world.

events.name: Mentor Session: Shan Boodram (Arya)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Storytelling, Content Strategy/Production.

events.name: Money, Sex & Religion - How Design Turns Taboo to Touchpoint
events.description: Sex, money, and religion are no longer taboo—they’re trending. This panel explores how behavioral UX, gamification, and human-centered design can destigmatize what used to be off-limits. From intimacy apps to investing platforms, discover how intentional design is helping users navigate vulnerable moments with confidence. Hear from sex educator Shan Boodram, Alinea’s Gen Z fintech founder Anam Lakhani, Bloomberg’s Riley Griffin, and Play Ventures’ Phylicia Koh—one of few female investors in gaming VC—on how intentional UX is turning discomfort into discovery.

events.name: Building the Alien: Morphology, Mutation & Future Life
events.description: What if we didn’t just search for alien life—but built it? Drawing from Proxima Kósmos, a research project exploring how life and intelligence might emerge under radically different conditions, this session examines the intersection of scientific experimentation and design. Claire Webb and designer Shane Griffin present Xenoterra—a modeled world inspired by evolutionary robotics and AI—that reimagines how we define life itself.

events.name: Code Switch: Defending Black EdTech amid AI Crackdowns
events.venue: The Westin Austin Downtown - Paramount I-II
events.description: As AI takes center stage and political resistance to equity accelerates, Black-centered EdTech stands at a crossroads. This panel spotlights philanthropic, nonprofit, product, and platform leaders working to protect the cultural, pedagogical, and digital infrastructure of Black EdTech. One year into new AI regulations and increasing DEI rollbacks, we’ll explore how to build for resilience – embedding protections into platforms, pedagogy, and policy. What does it take not just to survive, but to lead and thrive in the face of erasure?

events.name: Mentor Session: Shawn DuBravac (Avrio Institute)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Storytelling, Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Future of Work.

events.name: Zero-Gravity, Zero Barriers: Future of Inclusive Spaceflight
events.description: Discover what true Space accessibility means at this groundbreaking SXSW panel. Experts—including the first deaf Asian female pilot and aspiring astronaut, the founder of the Blind Institute of Technology, and a Space leader championing disability inclusion—reveal what it will take to make Space open to all. Explore innovations in pilot training, zero-gravity research, AI-assisted interfaces, and adaptive prosthetics. Learn how inclusive Space design inspires broader accessibility on Earth, reshaping our vision of who belongs in the final frontier.

events.name: Mentor Session: Shelley Gretlein (FARO Technologies)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Business Development, Content Strategy/Production, "Diversity, Equity & Inclusion", Marketing/Advertising/PR, Storytelling.

events.name: Meet Your New Work Team: AI, Chatbots, and the Workplace
events.description: "Hello, how can I help you?" Standard questions that once started human conversations are now chatbot prompts. As AI embeds in workplaces worldwide, how are these tools transforming professional interactions and workplace culture? What risks are encoded in systems mediating communication between colleagues and customers? This panel examines the EU's framework for compliant AI deployment, emerging hiring trends, and shifting skill demands. From voice interfaces to decision-making algorithms, discover how workplace AI is being designed-and what it means for workers, employers, and society.

events.name: Data Equity Core Conversation
events.description: As data becomes the raw material of modern life, how do we ensure it's collected, shared, and used in ways that build, not break, trust? This structured meet-up invites technologists , policymakers, and community advocates to explore responsible data practices across industries. Together, we’ll reflect on the disappearance of public data, rising mistrust in institutions, and what it means to build for integrity, transparency, and equity. Participants will share challenges and insights, contribute to a collective best practices, and connect with others shaping the future of ethical data use.

events.name: Mentor Session: Simone Kliass (XRBR (Brazilian Extended-Reality Association))
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Entrepreneurship, Storytelling, Immersive Technology.

events.name: A Guide to the SXSW 2026 XR Experience
events.description: Step into the future with three XR and SXSW veterans as they pull back the curtain on the special 10th anniversary edition of the XR Experience, a SXSW highlight since 2016. Deep dive into cutting-edge immersive tech, storytelling, and AI innovations. Gain exclusive insights into this year’s standout projects and discover how XR and AI are reshaping industries like entertainment, healthcare, retail, and education. Whether you’re a first-timer or a seasoned attendee, learn how to make the most of your time at the exhibition and connect with creators and industry leaders.

events.name: 25 Minutes of AI: The SXSW Speed-Briefing
events.venue: The Thompson Hotel Austin - Red River Ballroom
events.description:
The AI news cycle doesn’t just move fast—it moves at the speed of culture. In this high-intensity, 25-minute live briefing, Monks takes their signature monthly innovation series from the screen to the stage. This isn't a lecture; it’s a no-nonsense sprint through the breakthroughs, the hype, and the real-world applications actually worth your time.

Designed for the SXSW audience, this session is structured into four rapid-fire acts:
Monthly Wonders: A pulse check on the week’s biggest AI news and hidden gems surfacing in Austin.
The 101 Deep Dive: We peel back the curtain on a complex concept—moving beyond the buzzwords to explain the tech shifting the industry.
Monks in Action: A raw, behind-the-scenes walkthrough of an AI-powered case study, demonstrating how we turn agentic workflows into creative impact.
The Homework: You leave with a tangible task—a specific tool to test or a framework to apply—ensuring you walk out smarter than you walked in.

events.name: Robots to Reality: AI Logistics Shaping Consumer Lives
events.description:
What happens when AI moves beyond the hype and starts transforming the entire journey, from supply chain operations to the customer experience? 
We’ll explore how AI and robotics eliminate friction, anticipate customer needs, and turn operational and behavioral data into faster deliveries, greater transparency, and a seamless, elevated customer experience.

name: Stella Offner
title: Professor of Astronomy, Director NSF-Simons AI Institute for Cosmic Origins
company: The University of Texas at Austin

events.name: Revolutionizing Astronomy with Next Generation Big Data
events.description: Massive datasets, like those in astronomy, are the lifeblood of AI innovation.  The panel, led by researchers from the NSF-Simons AI Institute for Cosmic Origins, will discuss how AI is transforming astronomy to unlock new insights into the origins of our universe. They will present visualizations showing state-of-the-art astronomical datasets built on flagship observations and supercomputing simulations of the Universe. They will discuss how these data can be used to develop novel AI methods that accelerate discovery, expanding the frontiers of AI possibility inside and outside astronomy.

events.name: Revolutionizing Astronomy with Next Generation Big Data
events.description: Massive datasets, like those in astronomy, are the lifeblood of AI innovation.  The panel, led by researchers from the NSF-Simons AI Institute for Cosmic Origins, will discuss how AI is transforming astronomy to unlock new insights into the origins of our universe. They will present visualizations showing state-of-the-art astronomical datasets built on flagship observations and supercomputing simulations of the Universe. They will discuss how these data can be used to develop novel AI methods that accelerate discovery, expanding the frontiers of AI possibility inside and outside astronomy.

events.name: Building AI Pathways for HBCU Talent & Community Impact
events.venue: Austin Marriott Downtown - Waller Ballroom D
events.description: As AI rapidly reshapes education and work, HBCUs sit at a critical intersection of talent development, cultural stewardship, and community accountability. This panel brings together higher education, industry, and ecosystem leaders to examine how ethical AI pathways can be built with HBCUs—not around them. Panelists will explore practical models for aligning curriculum, workforce pipelines, industry partnerships, and community impact so AI innovation leads to access, ownership, and long-term opportunity for Black talent and communities.

events.name: The Future of Truth: How AI Reshapes Reality
events.description: Truth has never been straightforward. But now, AI offers to solve it—filter the noise, rank the sources, settle the debate. We’re told it’ll help us see clearly across politics, science, work, art, and justice. But what if the answers it gives us come with hidden terms? In this talk, Steven Rosenbaum unpacks the uneasy promise of algorithmic truth—and what’s lost when machines start shaping what counts as real. The Future of Truth: How AI Shapes Reality.

events.name: Early Days Again: What AI Makes Possible for New Schools
events.description:
AI tools are improving quickly, and educators are already finding meaningful uses for them. What hasn’t been defined yet is the school model that makes AI work for students and teachers in a coherent, reliable way at scale.

This session examines what early adopters of AI in public education are learning in real classrooms and school systems, the concrete design choices and conditions behind early success, and the operational, instructional, and governance questions that must be resolved over the next five years for AI to strengthen public education rather than fragment it.

events.name: Smart Talks Live: Brewing Smarter with HEINEKEN and IBM
events.description: How is HEINEKEN transforming itself into the world’s best‑connected brewer? Malcolm Gladwell and HEINEKEN’s Chief AI Officer, Surajeet Ghosh, sit down for a live episode of Smart Talks with IBM podcast to explore how the company is using data, AI and strong partnerships to create better experiences for customers and consumers worldwide. Hear how HEINEKEN is modernizing its digital backbone, empowering its people, and unlocking new ways to innovate in the future of beer.

name: Susan Reed
title: Director of IT Solutions / Acting Division Head of IT / Deputy Division Head of IT

events.name: Innovating in Science & Tech When Budgets are Tight
events.description: When public funding becomes unpredictable, how can organizations boost efficiency without stifling innovation? This fireside chat explores how the Space Telescope Science Institute, home of the Hubble and Webb telescopes, revolutionized access to space data. The lessons, born before today’s budget pressures, offer a powerful blueprint for any organization facing resource constraints. Whether you're advancing science, education, or social impact, the message is clear: Transformation is possible, even in lean times.

events.name: Code Switch: Defending Black EdTech amid AI Crackdowns
events.venue: The Westin Austin Downtown - Paramount I-II
events.description: As AI takes center stage and political resistance to equity accelerates, Black-centered EdTech stands at a crossroads. This panel spotlights philanthropic, nonprofit, product, and platform leaders working to protect the cultural, pedagogical, and digital infrastructure of Black EdTech. One year into new AI regulations and increasing DEI rollbacks, we’ll explore how to build for resilience – embedding protections into platforms, pedagogy, and policy. What does it take not just to survive, but to lead and thrive in the face of erasure?

events.name: From Policy to Practice: AI Implementation in Government
events.description: Artificial intelligence is no longer theoretical. Across the country, governments are moving from conversations about AI governance to real-world deployment — modernizing services, strengthening cybersecurity, improving legislative analysis, and rethinking how the public sector operates. But implementation is where the real work begins.

events.name: Designing the Future of Human Performance
events.description:
What happens when elite athleticism meets cutting-edge technology? This session explores how human performance, innovation, and storytelling converge to push the boundaries of what’s possible in sport and beyond.

A conversation with Paralympian Hunter Woodhall, Olympic Gold medalist Tara Davis-Woodhall, Deloitte’s US Sports Chief Business Officer Anthony Stephan, and Narrative principal Justin Moore-Lewy.

events.name: Featured Session: Real-World Impact of AI: Opportunities, Risks and Intellectual Property Oh My!
events.description: AI is the most transformative technology of our generation. This session explores the promise and perils of AI, sharing concrete examples of how AI is being applied with impact by businesses, creators and others to drive operational efficiency, enhance customer experiences, increase revenues and augment creativity. The audience will also learn about the risks associated with AI including some novel intellectual property issues (IP) posed, and how to weigh opportunities with risks. The insights shared are from a speaker who has built AI solutions and been at the top levels of technology, law, business and government and boardroom, all of which perspectives are critical to the successful AI applications.

events.name: Designing AI for the Worst Day of Your Life
events.description: Would you trust AI to save your life? Join Motorola Solutions and Anthropic to explore designing AI for your worst day. Discover how rigorously boundary testing AI in life-or-death situations—where seconds save lives—reveals critical truths for AI safety. Unpack "good friction" and essential controls to prevent harmful AI, including automation bias. Learn frameworks for responsible human-AI collaboration applicable across any industry, building AI you can stake your life on.

events.name: Code Switch: Defending Black EdTech amid AI Crackdowns
events.venue: The Westin Austin Downtown - Paramount I-II
events.description: As AI takes center stage and political resistance to equity accelerates, Black-centered EdTech stands at a crossroads. This panel spotlights philanthropic, nonprofit, product, and platform leaders working to protect the cultural, pedagogical, and digital infrastructure of Black EdTech. One year into new AI regulations and increasing DEI rollbacks, we’ll explore how to build for resilience – embedding protections into platforms, pedagogy, and policy. What does it take not just to survive, but to lead and thrive in the face of erasure?

events.name: Featured Session: Reclaiming our Humanity in the Age of AI
events.description: Too often, AI feels like something that’s done to us, not with us or for us. It will affect every aspect of our lives. But AI is neither poison nor panacea. It is and will be what we make it. Now is the moment to shape the future of AI for people. This panel will explore how we build the ecosystem and infrastructure to ensure that people will benefit the most from AI. Experts in AI, philanthropy, and advocacy, will offer a new way of thinking about AI that moves from Silicon Valley’s mantra of “move fast and break things” to an alternative vision that centers our humanity.

events.name: The Future of Development: Anthropic x Bolt.new A Case Study
events.description: Bolt.new went from $0 to $40M ARR in five months after launching on Claude 3.5 Sonnet — making it the fastest-growing customer in Anthropic's history. In this fireside chat, Bolt.new's CEO and CTO join Anthropic's Head of Applied AI to explore how AI is reshaping software development. They'll discuss the rise of "vibe coding," whether learning to code still matters, how the developer role is evolving from implementer to architect, and what's next as enterprise adoption accelerates. Expect candid takes on AI hype, scaling laws, technical debt, and what transformative AI actually looks like in production.

name: Tobias Peyerl
title: Head Of Strategic Intelligence And Analysis

events.name: The Great Recalibration: Centering Humanity in the Age of AI
events.description:
AI is rapidly reshaping how we work, create, and connect—yet at the same time, people are craving the tactile, the real, and the human. As AI-generated content floods our world, a new question emerges: where do we fit?

This panel explores the tension between acceleration and authenticity. How do we design AI that strengthens community instead of replacing it? How do we protect meaning, creativity, and trust in an era of misinformation and automation?

Innovation leaders will examine the cultural shift already underway and share a vision for an AI-powered future where human values and relationships remain at the center.

events.name: Building the Next Generation of Trustworthy AI: From Generic Cloud Models to Custom, Local Intelligence
events.description: AI adoption is growing, but cloud-based one-size-fits-all models are hitting limits — high costs, privacy concerns, environmental damage, & latency issues. This session will explore why the next generation of AI will be specialized, expert, & local. Panelists will unpack how custom models, trained on proprietary data and deployed closer to the user, can deliver improved performance, privacy, & scalability with a more sustainable infrastructure. They'll share how this approach is reshaping businesses and privacy expectations — and what it takes to make it widespread.

events.name: Reigniting STEM Interest: A 7,700-Student Study
events.venue: Austin Marriott Downtown - Waller Ballroom B
events.description: In an era of global challenges—from clean water to AI ethics—we need an avalanche of young innovators, yet STEM interest drops during the middle-to-high school transition. A 7,700-student study reveals 6 levers that raise engagement: meaningful experiences, parent influence, unlocking girls’ participation, courage/agency, clear career images, and reframing difficulty. Take away a shared language, tested patterns, and next steps for applying them through Activation → Identity → Career.

events.name: From Policy to Practice: AI Implementation in Government
events.description: Artificial intelligence is no longer theoretical. Across the country, governments are moving from conversations about AI governance to real-world deployment — modernizing services, strengthening cybersecurity, improving legislative analysis, and rethinking how the public sector operates. But implementation is where the real work begins.

events.name: How We Could Lose Control: Avoiding the Paths to Runaway AI
events.description: When experts talk about AI risks, they often talk about the loss of human control – but what does that actually mean? And how could it happen? In this fireside chat, physicist Anthony Aguirre and tech ethicist Tristan Harris go beyond surface-level alarm to map out the real mechanisms that could lead humanity to be sidelined by its own creation. From opaque incentives and design choices to systemic misalignment and power concentration, they’ll unpack how humans could lose control over AI and what can still be done to prevent it.

events.name: Can Media Survive AI? The Fight for Public Trust
events.description: AI is reshaping how information is created, distributed, and trusted. But it’s not just a threat - it’s an opportunity to reimagine what media can be. We will explore how AI is transforming business models, content incentives, and consumer expectations. From the rise of creators to the reinvention of trust, we’ll unpack what’s breaking, what’s possible, and how to build what’s next.

events.name: Brazil Creative Economy Meet Up
events.description: Brazil has one of the most innovative creative economies in the world. It is influenced by its multicultural background, versatility and diversity that shape and inspire its creative economy ecosystem. Come meet some of Brazil's communication professionals, audio-visual producers, designers, publishers, brands and startups, where a fluid exchange of ideas, business models and collaborations will take place. This is a place of encounter to develop a mutual and beneficial culture of exchange between all SXSW attendees and the Brazilian creative community.

events.name: AI, Your Brain & the Battle for Cognitive Sovereignty
events.description: AI tools rewrite emails, generate ideas, even decide what we see and when. But will they make us lazier thinkers—or force us to think beyond? Will it make us think more clearly or manipulate our perspectives? Will it widen the cognitive gap between rich and poor? Are our neuro-rights protected by design—or will screen time just be optimized again? In this live debate, you’ll pick a side: is AI expanding or eroding our minds? Expect sharp provocations, real-time participation, and one rule: you can’t outsource your thinking here. Join the battle for cognitive sovereignty.

events.name: AI Safety & Trust: Building Responsible Media Tech
events.description: As AI becomes central to media and entertainment, safety and trust are non-negotiable. This session explores practical steps for building and scaling trustworthy AI, including model testing, transparency, and regulatory compliance.

name: Vanessa Smith
title: ServiceNow Chief Corporate Affairs Officer and President, ServiceNow.org

The lessons, born before today’s budget pressures, offer a powerful blueprint for any organization facing resource constraints. Whether you're advancing science, education, or social impact, the message is clear: Transformation is possible, even in lean times.

events.name: Mentor Session: Vanya Petrovic (Coca-Cola EP)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning, Entrepreneurship, Future of Work.

events.name: A More Personal Computer: How AI is Transforming Desktops
events.description: In this session, MacPaw CTO Vira Tkachenko will explore how AI is redefining the concept of the “personal computer,” offering a more intuitive, private, and personalized experience. Drawing on her experience developing for the Apple ecosystem for 17+ years, she will share insights on building AI for macOS. Vira will trace the journey of Macs from their inception and highlight AI trends in software taking place across the industry. Attendees can learn how on-device intelligence enhances productivity, respects user privacy, and keeps the Mac a powerful tool for focused, meaningful work.

events.name: The End of SDV: Building an AI-Defined Vehicle
events.description: We’ve spent a decade making cars "smartphones on wheels," but the next era is about AI-defined mobility. And while the industry talks about "software-defined vehicles," the real breakthrough lies in the marriage of high-performance compute and intelligent systems. This conversation explores how Rivian is building a proprietary hardware-software stack to create vehicles that don't just react to the world, but actively anticipate the needs of the adventurer. We’re moving beyond "smart" features toward a world where the car’s local processing power drives performance and supports safety features and presence, even at the edge of the map.

events.name: Mentor Session: Yangos Hadjiyannis (Northeastern University Vancouver)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in XR, Storytelling, Immersive Technology, "Diversity, Equity & Inclusion".

name: Zach Marshall
title: Apple Pay Operations Manager/Professor
company: Apple/The University of Texas at Austin

events.name: Mentor Session: Zach Marshall (Apple/The University of Texas at Austin)
events.description: Meeting with this mentor is highly recommended if you're interested in Entrepreneurship, Fintech.

events.name: A.I. News That’s Fit to Print
events.description: In adopting artificial intelligence, news organizations are making starkly different choices about when and how to use the technology. Which is great, because we can compare and contrast each approach, to identify some early best practices. Zach Seward, who leads the A.I. Initiatives team at The New York Times, will present those findings across a wide range of compelling — and sometimes really terrible — uses of A.I. for journalism. He will also offer a look at how The Times is using A.I. to bolster the human expertise of its newsroom, while discovering new forms of reporting.
